text.NABRE: 19:6 When the Ammonites realized that they had put themselves in bad odor with David, Hanun and the Ammonites sent a thousand talents of silver to hire chariots and horsemen from Aram Naharaim, from Aram-maacah, and from Zobah.

text.NRSV: They hired thirty-two thousand chariots and the king of Maacah with his army, who came and encamped before Medeba. And the Ammonites were mustered from their cities and came to battle.
text.Rotherham: So they hired for themselves thirty-two thousand chariots, and the king of Maacah, and his people, who came and pitched before Medeba,—and the sons of Ammon gathered themselves together out of their cities, and entered into the war.
text.KJV: 19:7 So they hired thirtie and two thousand charets, and the king of Maachah and his people, who came and pitched before Medeba. And the children of Ammon gathered themselues together from their cities, and came to battaile.
text.NABRE: 19:7 They hired thirty-two thousand chariots along with the king of Maacah and his army, who came and encamped before Medeba. The Ammonites also assembled from their cities and came out for war.

text.NRSV: When David heard of it, he sent Joab and all the army of the warriors.
text.Rotherham: Now, when David heard, he sent Joab and all the army of heroes.
text.KJV: 19:8 And when Dauid heard of it, hee sent Ioab, and all the host of the mightie men.
text.NABRE: 19:8 When David heard of this, he sent Joab and his whole army of warriors against them.

text.NRSV: The Ammonites came out and drew up in battle array at the entrance of the city, and the kings who had come were by themselves in the open country.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Ammon came out, and set in array for the battle, at the opening of the city,—whereas, the kings who had come, were by themselves, in the field.
text.KJV: 19:9 And the children of Ammon came out, and put the battell in aray before the gate of the citie, and the kings that were come, were by themselues in the field.
text.NABRE: 19:9 The Ammonites marched out and lined up for battle at the entrance of the city, while the kings who had come to their help remained apart in the open field.

text.NRSV: When Joab saw that the line of battle was set against him both in front and in the rear, he chose some of the picked men of Israel and arrayed them against the Arameans;
text.Rotherham: So, when Joab saw that the battle confronted him, before and behind,—he chose out of all the choice in Israel, and set in array to meet the Syrians;
text.KJV: 19:10 Now when Ioab saw that the battell was set against him, before and behinde: hee chose out of all the choice of Israel, and put them in aray against the Syrians.
text.NABRE: 19:10 When Joab saw that there was a battle line both in front of and behind him, he chose some of the best fighters among the Israelites and lined them up against the Arameans;

text.NRSV: the rest of his troops he put in the charge of his brother Abishai, and they were arrayed against the Ammonites.
text.Rotherham: and, the rest of the people, delivered he into the hand of Abishai his brother,—and they set in array, to meet the sons of Ammon.
text.KJV: 19:11 And the rest of the people hee deliuered vnto the hand of Abishai his brother, and they set themselues in aray against the children of Ammon.
text.NABRE: 19:11 the rest of the army, which he placed under the command of his brother Abishai, then lined up to oppose the Ammonites.

text.NRSV: He said, “If the Arameans are too strong for me, then you shall help me; but if the Ammonites are too strong for you, then I will help you.
text.Rotherham: And he said—If, too strong for me, be the Syrians, then shalt thou come to my help,—but, if, the sons of Ammon, be too strong for thee, then will I help thee.
text.KJV: 19:12 And he said, If the Syrians bee too strong for me, then thou shalt helpe me: but if the children of Ammon be too strong for thee, then I wil helpe thee.
text.NABRE: 19:12 And he said: “If the Arameans prove too strong for me, you must come and save me; and if the Ammonites prove too strong for you, I will save you.

text.NRSV: Be brave, and let us fight bravely for our people and for the cities of our God; and may the Lord do what seems good to him.”
text.Rotherham: Be strong, and let us put forth our strength, for the sake of our people, and for the sake of the cities of our God,—and let, Yahweh, do, what is good in his own eyes.
text.KJV: 19:13 Be of good courage, and let vs behaue our selues valiantly for our people, and for the Cities of our God: and let the Lord do that which is good in his sight.
text.NABRE: 19:13 Hold firm and let us show ourselves courageous for the sake of our people and the cities of our God; and may the Lord do what is good in his sight.”

text.NRSV: So Joab and the troops who were with him advanced toward the Arameans for battle; and they fled before him.
text.Rotherham: Then did Joab, and all the people who were with him, draw near before the Syrians unto the battle,—and they fled from before him.
text.KJV: 19:14 So Ioab and the people that were with him, drew nigh before the Syrians, vnto the battell; and they fled before him.
text.NABRE: 19:14 Joab therefore advanced with his men to engage the Arameans in battle; but they fled before him.

text.NRSV: When the Ammonites saw that the Arameans fled, they likewise fled before Abishai, Joab’s brother, and entered the city. Then Joab came to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And, when, the sons of Ammon, saw that the Syrians had fled, then fled, they also, from before Abishai his brother, and entered the city,—and Joab came to Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 19:15 And when the children of Ammon saw that the Syrians were fled, they likewise fled before Abishai his brother, and entred into the city. Then Ioab came to Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: 19:15 And when the Ammonites saw that the Arameans had fled, they too fled before his brother Abishai, and entered their city. Joab then came to Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: But when the Arameans saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they sent messengers and brought out the Arameans who were beyond the Euphrates, with Shophach the commander of the army of Hadadezer at their head.
text.Rotherham: And when the Syrians saw that they had been defeated before Israel, then sent they messengers, and brought out the Syrians who were Beyond the River,—with Shophach captain of the army of Hadarezer, before them.
text.KJV: 19:16 ¶ And when the Syrians saw that they were put to the worse before Israel, they sent messengers, and drew forth the Syrians, that were beyond the Riuer: and Shophach the captaine of the hoste of Hadarezer, went before them.
text.NABRE: 19:16 Seeing themselves vanquished by Israel, the Arameans sent messengers to bring out the Arameans from beyond the Euphrates, with Shophach, the commander of Hadadezer’s army, at their head.

text.NRSV: When David was informed, he gathered all Israel together, crossed the Jordan, came to them, and drew up his forces against them. When David set the battle in array against the Arameans, they fought with him.
text.Rotherham: And it was told David, and he gathered together all Israel, and crossed the Jordan, and came unto them, and set in array against them,—and, when David had set in array against the Syrians for battle, they fought with him.
text.KJV: 19:17 And it was tolde Dauid, and hee gathered all Israel, and passed ouer Iordane, and came vpon them, and set the battell in aray against them: so when Dauid had put the battell in aray against the Syrians, they fought with him.
text.NABRE: 19:17 When this was reported to David, he gathered all Israel together, crossed the Jordan, and met them. With the army of David drawn up to fight the Arameans, they gave battle.

text.NRSV: The Arameans fled before Israel; and David killed of the Arameans seven thousand chariot-drivers and forty thousand foot soldiers, and also killed Shophach the commander of their army.
text.Rotherham: And the Syrians fled from before Israel, and David slew of the Syrians [the men of] seven thousand chariots, and forty thousand footmen,—and, Shophach captain of the army, he put to death.
text.KJV: 19:18 But the Syrians fled before Israel, and Dauid slew of the Syrians seuen thousand men, which fought in charets, and fourty thousand footmen, and killed Shophach the captaine of the hoste.
text.NABRE: 19:18 But the Arameans fled before Israel, and David killed seven thousand of their chariot fighters and forty thousand of their foot soldiers; he also put to death Shophach, the commander of the army.

text.NRSV: When the servants of Hadadezer saw that they had been defeated by Israel, they made peace with David, and became subject to him. So the Arameans were not willing to help the Ammonites any more.
text.Rotherham: And, when the servants of Hadarezer saw that they were defeated before Israel, they made peace with David and served him,—neither would the Syrians help the sons of Ammon any more.
text.KJV: 19:19 And when the seruants of Hadarezer saw that they were put to the worse before Israel, they made peace with Dauid, and became his seruants: neither would the Syrians helpe the children of Ammon any more.
text.NABRE: 19:19 When the vassals of Hadadezer saw themselves vanquished by Israel, they made peace with David and became his subjects. After this, the Arameans refused to come to the aid of the Ammonites.

text.NRSV: In the spring of the year, the time when kings go out to battle, Joab led out the army, ravaged the country of the Ammonites, and came and besieged Rabbah. But David remained at Jerusalem. Joab struck Rabbah, and overthrew it.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, at the time of the turn of the year, at the time of the going forth of kings, that Joab led forth the force of the army, and laid waste the land of the sons of Ammon, and came and besieged Rabbah, but, David, was tarrying in Jerusalem,—and Joab smote Rabbah, and overthrew it.
text.KJV: 20:1 And it came to passe, that after the yeere was expired, at the time that kings goe out to battell, Ioab led forth the power of the armie, and wasted the countrey of the children of Ammon, and came and besieged Rabbah (but Dauid taried at Ierusalem,) and Ioab smote Rabbah, and destroyed it.
text.NABRE: 20:1 Chapter 20 At the turn of the year, the time when kings go to war, Joab led the army out in force, laid waste the land of the Ammonites, and went on to besiege Rabbah; David himself remained in Jerusalem. When Joab had attacked Rabbah and destroyed it,

text.NRSV: David took the crown of Milcom from his head; he found that it weighed a talent of gold, and in it was a precious stone; and it was placed on David’s head. He also brought out the booty of the city, a very great amount.
text.Rotherham: And David took the crown of their king from off his head, and found it weighed a talent of gold, and, therein, were precious stones, and it was [set] on the head of David,—and, the spoil of the city, brought he forth, in great abundance;
text.KJV: 20:2 And Dauid tooke the crowne of their king from off his head, and found it to weigh a talent of gold, and there were precious stones in it, and it was set vpon Dauids head; and hee brought also exceeding much spoile out of the city.
text.NABRE: 20:2 David took the crown of Milcom from the idol’s head. It was found to weigh a talent of gold, with precious stones on it; this crown David wore on his own head. He also brought out a great amount of spoil from the city.

text.NRSV: He brought out the people who were in it, and set them to work with saws and iron picks and axes. Thus David did to all the cities of the Ammonites. Then David and all the people returned to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: and, the people that were therein, brought he forth, and cut with the saw and with threshing sledges of iron and with axes, thus also, used David to do unto all the cities of the son of Ammon,—and David and all the people returned to Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 20:3 And hee brought out the people that were in it, and cut them with sawes, and with harrowes of yron, and with axes: euen so dealt Dauid with all the cities of the children of Ammon. And Dauid and all the people returned to Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: 20:3 He deported the people of the city and set them to work with saws, iron picks, and axes. David dealt thus with all the cities of the Ammonites. Then David and his whole army returned to Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: After this, war broke out with the Philistines at Gezer; then Sibbecai the Hushathite killed Sippai, who was one of the descendants of the giants; and the Philistines were subdued.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after this, that there was yet again a battle in Gezer, with the Philistines,—then, Sibbecai the Hushathite smote Sippai of the children of the giants, and they were subdued.
text.KJV: 20:4 ¶ And it came to passe after this, that there arose warre at Gezer with the Philistines, at which time Sibbechai the Hushathite, slew Sippai, that was of the children of the giant: and they were subdued.
text.NABRE: 20:4 Victories over the Philistines. Afterward there was another battle with the Philistines, at Gezer. At that time, Sibbecai the Hushathite struck down Sippai, one of the descendants of the Rephaim, and the Philistines were subdued.

text.NRSV: Again there was war with the Philistines; and Elhanan son of Jair killed Lahmi the brother of Goliath the Gittite, the shaft of whose spear was like a weaver’s beam.
text.Rotherham: And there again came to be a battle with the Philistines,—and Elhanan son of Jair smote Lahmi, brother of Goliath the Gittite, the staff of whose spear, was like a weaver’s beam.
text.KJV: 20:5 And there was warre againe with the Philistines, and Elhanan the sonne of Iair, slew Lahmi the brother of Goliath the Gittite, whose spearestaffe was like a weauers beame.
text.NABRE: 20:5 There was another battle with the Philistines, and Elhanan, the son of Jair, slew Lahmi, the brother of Goliath of Gath, whose spear shaft was like a weaver’s beam.

text.NRSV: Again there was war at Gath, where there was a man of great stature, who had six fingers on each hand, and six toes on each foot, twenty-four in number; he also was descended from the giants.
text.Rotherham: And there again came to be war with Gath,—where was a man of great stature, whose fingers and toes, six on each hand and foot, were four and twenty, and, he also, had been born unto the giant.
text.KJV: 20:6 And yet againe there was warre at Gath, where was a man of great stature, whose fingers and toes were foure and twentie, sixe on each hand, and sixe on each foot. And he also was the sonne of the giant.
text.NABRE: 20:6 There was another battle, at Gath, and there was a giant, who had six fingers to each hand and six toes to each foot; twenty-four in all. He too was descended from the Rephaim.

text.NRSV: When he defied Israel, Jonathan son of Shimea, David’s brother, killed him.
text.Rotherham: But, when he reproached Israel, Jonathan, son of Shimea brother of David, smote him.
text.KJV: 20:7 But when he defied Israel, Ionathan the sonne of Shimea Dauids brother, slew him.
text.NABRE: 20:7 He defied Israel, and Jonathan, the son of Shimea, David’s brother, slew him.

text.NRSV: These were descended from the giants in Gath; they fell by the hand of David and his servants.
text.Rotherham: These, were born unto the giant in Gath,—but they fell by the hand of David, and by the hand of his servants.
text.KJV: 20:8 These were borne vnto the Giant in Gath, and they fell by the hand of Dauid, and by the hand of his seruants.
text.NABRE: 20:8 These were the descendants of the Rephaim of Gath who died at the hands of David and his servants.

text.NRSV: Satan stood up against Israel, and incited David to count the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Then the accuser stood up, against Israel,—and moved David, to number Israel.
text.KJV: 21:1 And Satan stoode vp against Israel, and prouoked Dauid to number Israel.
text.NABRE: 21:1 Chapter 21 David’s Census; the Plague. A satan rose up against Israel, and he incited David to take a census of Israel.

text.NRSV: So David said to Joab and the commanders of the army, “Go, number Israel, from Beer-sheba to Dan, and bring me a report, so that I may know their number.”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto Joab and unto the rulers of the people, Go, number Israel, from Beer-sheba even unto Dan,—and bring [the report] unto me, that I may know the number of them.
text.KJV: 21:2 And Dauid saide to Ioab, and to the rulers of the people, Goe, number Israel from Beer-sheba eueu to Dan: and bring the number of them to me, that I may know it.
text.NABRE: 21:2 David therefore said to Joab and to the other generals of the army, “Go, number the Israelites from Beer-sheba to Dan, and report back to me that I may know their number.”

text.NRSV: But Joab said, “May the Lord increase his people a hundred times! Are they not, my lord the king, all of them my lord’s servants? Why then should my lord require this? Why should he bring guilt on Israel?”
text.Rotherham: Then said Joab, May Yahweh add unto his people, as many as they are, a hundred times, but are they not, my lord O king, all of them my lord’s, as servants? wherefore should my lord seek this? wherefore should it become guilt to Israel?
text.KJV: 21:3 And Ioab answered, The Lord make his people an hundred times so many moe as they bee: but, my lord the king, are they not al my lords seruants? why then doeth my lord require this thing? why will hee bee a cause of trespasse to Israel?
text.NABRE: 21:3 But Joab replied: “May the Lord increase his people a hundredfold! My lord king, are not all of them my lord’s subjects? Why does my lord seek to do this thing? Why should he bring guilt upon Israel?”

text.NRSV: But the king’s word prevailed against Joab. So Joab departed and went throughout all Israel, and came back to Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: But, the word of the king, prevailed against Joab,—and Joab departed, and went to and fro throughout all Israel, and came to Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 21:4 Neuerthelesse, the kings word preuailed against Ioab: wherefore Ioab departed, and went throughout all Israel, and came to Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: 21:4 However, the king’s command prevailed over Joab, who departed and traversed all of Israel, and then returned to Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Joab gave the total number of the people to David. In all Israel there were one million one hundred thousand men who drew the sword, and in Judah four hundred seventy thousand who drew the sword.
text.Rotherham: And Joab delivered the sum of the number of the people, unto David,—and all Israel was found to be a thousand thousand and a hundred thousand men, who drew sword, and Judah, four hundred and seventy thousand men, who drew sword.
text.KJV: 21:5 ¶ And Ioab gaue the summe of the number of the people vnto Dauid: and all they of Israel were a thousand thousand, and an hundred thousand men that drew sword: and Iudah was foure hundred threescore and ten thousand men, that drew sword.
text.NABRE: 21:5 Joab reported the census figures to David: of men capable of wielding a sword, there were in all Israel one million one hundred thousand, and in Judah four hundred and seventy thousand.

text.NRSV: But he did not include Levi and Benjamin in the numbering, for the king’s command was abhorrent to Joab.
text.Rotherham: But, Levi and Benjamin, counted he not among them,—for detestable was the word of the king unto Joab.
text.KJV: 21:6 But Leui and Beniamin counted hee not among them: for the kings word was abominable to Ioab.
text.NABRE: 21:6 Levi and Benjamin, however, he did not include in the census, for the king’s command was repugnant to Joab.

text.NRSV: But God was displeased with this thing, and he struck Israel.
text.Rotherham: And it was displeasing in the eyes of God, concerning this thing,—therefore smote he Israel.
text.KJV: 21:7 And God was displeased with this thing, therefore he smote Israel.
text.NABRE: 21:7 This command was evil in the sight of God, and he struck Israel.

text.NRSV: David said to God, “I have sinned greatly in that I have done this thing. But now, I pray you, take away the guilt of your servant; for I have done very foolishly.”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto God, I have sinned greatly, in that I have done this thing,—but, now, I beseech thee, take away the iniquity of thy servant, for I have shown myself very foolish.
text.KJV: 21:8 And Dauid saide vnto God, I haue sinned greatly, because I haue done this thing: but uow, I beseech thee, doe away the iniquitie of thy seruant, for I haue done very foolishly.
text.NABRE: 21:8 Then David said to God, “I have sinned greatly in doing this thing. Take away your servant’s guilt, for I have acted very foolishly.”

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Gad, David’s seer, saying,
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Gad, the seer of David saying:
text.KJV: 21:9 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Gad, Dauids Seer, saying,
text.NABRE: 21:9 Then the Lord spoke to Gad, David’s seer, in these words:

text.NRSV: “Go and say to David: Thus says the Lord: Three things I offer you; choose one of them, so that I may do it to you.”
text.Rotherham: Go—and thou shalt speak unto David, saying, Thus, saith Yahweh, Three things, do I offer thee,—choose thee one from among them, that I may do it unto thee.
text.KJV: 21:10 Goe and tell Dauid, saying, Thus saith the Lord, I offer thee three things, choose thee one of them, that I may doe it vnto thee.
text.NABRE: 21:10 Go, tell David: Thus says the Lord: I am laying out three options; choose one of them, and I will inflict it on you.

text.NRSV: So Gad came to David and said to him, “Thus says the Lord: Take your choice:
text.Rotherham: So Gad came unto David,—and said to him, Thus, saith Yahweh,
text.KJV: 21:11 So Gad came to Dauid, and said vnto him, Thus saith the Lord, Choose thee
text.NABRE: 21:11 Accordingly, Gad went to David and said to him: “Thus says the Lord: Decide now—

text.NRSV: either three years of famine; or three months of devastation by your foes, while the sword of your enemies overtakes you; or three days of the sword of the Lord, pestilence on the land, and the angel of the Lord destroying throughout all the territory of Israel. Now decide what answer I shall return to the one who sent me.”
text.Rotherham: Choose thee:—Whether, for three years, [there be] famine, Or, for three months, that thou flee before thine adversaries, while the sword of thine enemies overtaketh thee, Or, for three days, the sword of Yahweh, and pestilence be in the land, with, the messenger of Yahweh, laying waste throughout all the boundary of Israel,—Now, therefore, see, what, answer, I shall return unto him that sent me.
text.KJV: 21:12 Either three yeeres famine, or three moneths to bee destroyed before thy foes (while that the sword of thine enemies ouertaketh thee) or else three dayes the sword of the Lord, euen the pestilence in the land, and the Angel of the Lord destroying throughout all the coasts of Israel. Now therefore aduise thy selfe, what word I shall bring againe to him that sent me.
text.NABRE: 21:12 will it be three years of famine; or three months of fleeing your enemies, with the sword of your foes ever at your back; or three days of the Lord ’s own sword, a plague in the land, with the Lord ’s destroying angel in every part of Israel? Now consider: What answer am I to give him who sent me?”

text.NRSV: Then David said to Gad, “I am in great distress; let me fall into the hand of the Lord, for his mercy is very great; but let me not fall into human hands.”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto Gad, I am in a great strait,—Oh let me fall, I pray thee, into the hand of Yahweh, for abounding are his compassions, exceedingly, but, into the hand of man, let me not fall.
text.KJV: 21:13 And Dauid said vnto Gad, I am in a great strait. Let mee fall now into the hand of the Lord (for very great are his mercies,) but let me not fall into the hand of man.
text.NABRE: 21:13 Then David said to Gad: “I am in serious trouble. But let me fall into the hand of the Lord, whose mercy is very great, rather than into hands of men.”

text.NRSV: So the Lord sent a pestilence on Israel; and seventy thousand persons fell in Israel.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh laid pestilence, upon Israel,—and there fell, of Israel, seventy thousand men.
text.KJV: 21:14 ¶ So the Lord sent pestilence vpon Israel: and there fell of Israel, seuentie thousand men.
text.NABRE: 21:14 Therefore the Lord sent a plague upon Israel, and seventy thousand Israelites died.

text.NRSV: And God sent an angel to Jerusalem to destroy it; but when he was about to destroy it, the Lord looked and relented concerning the calamity; he said to the destroying angel, “Enough! Stay your hand.” The angel of the Lord was then standing by the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.
text.Rotherham: And God sent a messenger to Jerusalem, to lay it waste, but, as he was laying it waste, Yahweh looked, and relented concerning the calamity, and said unto the messenger who was laying waste, Enough! now, stay thy hand. And, the messenger of Yahweh, was standing by the threshing-floor of Ornan the Jebusite.
text.KJV: 21:15 And God sent an Angel vnto Ierusalem to destroy it: and as he was destroying, the Lord beheld, and he repented him of the euill, and said to the Angel that destroyed, It is ynough, stay now thine hand. And the Angel of the Lord stood by the threshing floore of Ornan the Iebusite.
text.NABRE: 21:15 God also sent an angel to Jerusalem to destroy it; but as the angel was on the point of destroying it, the Lord saw and changed his mind about the calamity, and said to the destroying angel, “Enough now! Stay your hand!” Ornan’s Threshing Floor. The angel of the Lord was then standing by the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.

text.NRSV: David looked up and saw the angel of the Lord standing between earth and heaven, and in his hand a drawn sword stretched out over Jerusalem. Then David and the elders, clothed in sackcloth, fell on their faces.
text.Rotherham: And, when David lifted up his eyes, and saw the messenger of Yahweh, standing, between the earth and the heavens, and his sword drawn, in his hand, stretched out over Jerusalem, then fell David and the elders, clothed in sackcloth, upon their faces.
text.KJV: 21:16 And Dauid lift vp his eyes, and saw the Angel of the Lord stand betweene the earth and the heauen, hauing a drawen sword in his hand stretched out ouer Ierusalem. Then Dauid and the Elders of Israel, who were clothed in sackecloth, fell vpon their faces.
text.NABRE: 21:16 When David raised his eyes, he saw the angel of the Lord standing between earth and heaven, drawn sword in hand stretched out against Jerusalem. David and the elders, clothed in sackcloth, fell face down,

text.NRSV: And David said to God, “Was it not I who gave the command to count the people? It is I who have sinned and done very wickedly. But these sheep, what have they done? Let your hand, I pray, O Lord my God, be against me and against my father’s house; but do not let your people be plagued!”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto God, Was it not, I, who gave word to number the people? Yea, I, it was who sinned and did the great wrong, what then had, these sheep, done? O Yahweh, my God, let thy hand, I beseech thee, be against me, and against the house of my father, but not against, thy people, that they should be plagued!
text.KJV: 21:17 And Dauid said vnto God, Is it not I that commanded the people to be numbred? euen I it is that haue sinned, and done euill indeed, but as for these sheepe, what haue they done? Let thine hand, I pray thee, O Lord my God, be on me, and on my fathers house, but not on thy people, that they should bee plagued.
text.NABRE: 21:17 and David prayed to God: “Was it not I who ordered the census of the people? I am the one who sinned, I did this wicked thing. But these sheep, what have they done? O Lord, my God, strike me and my father’s family, but do not afflict your people with this plague!”

text.NRSV: Then the angel of the Lord commanded Gad to tell David that David should go up and erect an altar to the Lord on the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.
text.Rotherham: And, the messenger of Yahweh, commanded Gad, to say unto David,—that David should go up, to rear an altar unto Yahweh, in the threshing-floor of Ornan the Jebusite.
text.KJV: 21:18 ¶ Then the Angel of the Lord commanded Gad to say to Dauid, that Dauid should goe vp and set vp an Altar vnto the Lord, in the threshing floore of Ornan the Iebusite.
text.NABRE: 21:18 Then the angel of the Lord commanded Gad to tell David to go up and set up an altar to the Lord on the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.

text.NRSV: So David went up at Gad’s word, which he had spoken in the name of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So David went up, according to the word of Gad, which he spake in the name of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 21:19 And Dauid went vp at the saying of Gad, which he spake in the Name of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 21:19 David went up at the word of Gad, which he spoke in the name of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Now Ornan turned and saw the angel; and while his four sons who were with him hid themselves, Ornan continued to thresh wheat.
text.Rotherham: And Ornan turned, and saw the messenger, but, his four sons with him, were hiding themselves,—now, Ornan, had been threshing wheat.
text.KJV: 21:20 And Ornan turned backe and saw the Angel, and his foure sonnes with him, hid themselues. Now Ornan was threshing wheat.
text.NABRE: 21:20 Ornan turned around and saw the king; his four sons who were with him hid themselves, but Ornan kept on threshing wheat.

text.NRSV: As David came to Ornan, Ornan looked and saw David; he went out from the threshing floor, and did obeisance to David with his face to the ground.
text.Rotherham: Then came David unto Ornan,—and Ornan looked, and saw David, and went forth out of the threshing-floor, and bowed himself unto David, with his face to the ground.
text.KJV: 21:21 And as Dauid came to Ornan, Ornan looked and saw Dauid, and went out of the threshing floore, and bowed himselfe to Dauid with his face to the ground.
text.NABRE: 21:21 But as David came toward Ornan, he looked up and saw that it was David, and left the threshing floor and bowed down before David, his face to the ground.

text.NRSV: David said to Ornan, “Give me the site of the threshing floor that I may build on it an altar to the Lord—give it to me at its full price—so that the plague may be averted from the people.”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto Ornan, Grant me the place of the threshing-floor, that I may build thereon an altar, unto Yahweh,—for silver in full, shalt thou grant it me, that the plague may be stayed from off the people.
text.KJV: 21:22 Then Dauid saide to Ornan, Grant mee the place of this threshing floore, that I may build an Altar therein vnto the Lord: thou shalt grant it mee for the full price, that the plague may be stayed from the people.
text.NABRE: 21:22 David said to Ornan: “Sell me the site of this threshing floor, that I may build on it an altar to the Lord. Sell it to me at its full price, that the plague may be withdrawn from the people.”

text.NRSV: Then Ornan said to David, “Take it; and let my lord the king do what seems good to him; see, I give the oxen for burnt offerings, and the threshing sledges for the wood, and the wheat for a grain offering. I give it all.”
text.Rotherham: And Ornan said unto David, Take it to thee, and let my lord the king do what is good in his own eyes,—see! I have given the oxen for ascending-sacrifices, and, the threshing implements for wood, and the wheat for a meal-offering,—the whole, have I given.
text.KJV: 21:23 And Ornan saide vnto Dauid, Take it to thee, and let my lord the king do that which is good in his eyes. Loe, I giue thee the oxen also for burnt offerings, and the threshing instruments for wood, and the wheat for the meate offering, I giue it all.
text.NABRE: 21:23 But Ornan said to David: “Take it as your own, and let my lord the king do what is good in his sight. See, I also give you the oxen for the burnt offerings, the threshing sledges for the wood, and the wheat for the grain offering. I give it all to you.”

text.NRSV: But King David said to Ornan, “No; I will buy it for the full price. I will not take for the Lord what is yours, nor offer burnt offerings that cost me nothing.”
text.Rotherham: Then said King David to Ornan, Nay! but I will, surely buy, it for silver in full,—for I will not take that which is thine for Yahweh, nor offer an ascending-sacrifice that hath cost me nothing.
text.KJV: 21:24 And king Dauid said to Ornan; Nay, but I wil verily buy it for the full price: for I will not take that which is thine for the Lord, nor offer burnt offerings without cost.
text.NABRE: 21:24 But King David replied to Ornan: “No! I will buy it from you properly, at its full price. I will not take what is yours for the Lord, nor bring burnt offerings that cost me nothing.”

text.NRSV: So David paid Ornan six hundred shekels of gold by weight for the site.
text.Rotherham: So David gave to Ornan, for the place,—shekels of gold, by weight, six hundred.
text.KJV: 21:25 So Dauid gaue to Ornan for the place, sixe hundred shekels of gold by weight.
text.NABRE: 21:25 So David paid Ornan six hundred shekels of gold for the place.

text.NRSV: David built there an altar to the Lord and presented burnt offerings and offerings of well-being. He called upon the Lord, and he answered him with fire from heaven on the altar of burnt offering.
text.Rotherham: And David built there an altar unto Yahweh, and caused to go up ascending-sacrifices, and peace-offerings,—and called unto Yahweh, and he responded to him, by fire, out of the heavens, upon the altar of ascending-sacrifice.
text.KJV: 21:26 And Dauid built there an Altar vnto the Lord, and offered burnt offerings, and peace offerings, and called vpon the Lord, and hee answered him from heauen by fire vpon the Altar of burnt offering.
text.NABRE: 21:26 Altar for Burnt Offerings. David then built an altar there to the Lord, and sacrificed burnt offerings and communion offerings. He called upon the Lord, who answered him by sending down fire from heaven upon the altar for burnt offerings.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord commanded the angel, and he put his sword back into its sheath.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh gave word to tile messenger, and he put back his sword into its sheath.
text.KJV: 21:27 And the Lord commaunded the Angel, and hee put vp his sword againe into the sheath thereof.
text.NABRE: 21:27 Then the Lord gave orders to the angel to return his sword to its sheath.

text.NRSV: At that time, when David saw that the Lord had answered him at the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite, he made his sacrifices there.
text.Rotherham: At that time, when David saw that Yahweh had answered him in the threshing-floor of Ornan the Jebusite, then he sacrificed there.
text.KJV: 21:28 ¶ At that time, when Dauid saw that the Lord had answered him in the threshing floore of Ornan the Iebusite, then he sacrificed there.
text.NABRE: 21:28 Once David saw that the Lord had answered him at the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite, he continued to offer sacrifices there.

text.NRSV: For the tabernacle of the Lord, which Moses had made in the wilderness, and the altar of burnt offering were at that time in the high place at Gibeon;
text.Rotherham: But, the habitation of Yahweh, which Moses made in the desert, and the altar of ascending-sacrifice, were, at that time, in the high place at Gibeon;
text.KJV: 21:29 For the tabernacle of the Lord which Moses made in the wildernesse, and the Altar of the burnt offering were at that season, in the high place at Gibeon:
text.NABRE: 21:29 The tabernacle of the Lord, which Moses had made in the wilderness, and the altar for burnt offerings were at that time on the high place at Gibeon.

text.NRSV: but David could not go before it to inquire of God, for he was afraid of the sword of the angel of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and David could not go before it, to seek God,—for he had been terrified, because of the sword of the messenger of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 21:30 But Dauid could not goe before it to euquire of God; for he was afraid, because of the sword of the Angel of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 21:30 But David could not go into his presence to inquire of God, for he was fearful of the sword of the angel of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Then David said, “Here shall be the house of the Lord God and here the altar of burnt offering for Israel.”
text.Rotherham: Then said David, This, is the house of Yahweh God,—and, this, is the altar of ascending-sacrifice, for Israel.
text.KJV: 22:1 Then Dauid said, This is the house of the Lord God, and this is the Altar of the burnt offering for Israel.
text.NABRE: 22:1 Chapter 22 Thus David said, “This is the house of the Lord God, and this is the altar for burnt offerings for Israel.”

text.NRSV: David gave orders to gather together the aliens who were in the land of Israel, and he set stonecutters to prepare dressed stones for building the house of God.
text.Rotherham: And David gave word, to gather together the sojourners, who were in the land of Israel,—and he set hewers to hew squared stones, for building the house of God;
text.KJV: 22:2 And Dauid commanded to gather together the strangers that were in the land of Israel: and hee set masons to hew wrought stones to build the house of God.
text.NABRE: 22:2 David then ordered that the resident aliens in the land of Israel should be brought together, and he appointed them stonecutters to hew out stone blocks for building the house of God.

text.NRSV: David also provided great stores of iron for nails for the doors of the gates and for clamps, as well as bronze in quantities beyond weighing,
text.Rotherham: and, iron in abundance, for nails for the doors of the gates, and for hooks, did David prepare,—and bronze in abundance, without weight;
text.KJV: 22:3 And Dauid prepared yron in abundance for the nailes for the doores of the gates, and for the ioynings, and brasse in abundance without weight;
text.NABRE: 22:3 David also laid up large stores of iron to make nails for the doors of the gates, and clamps, together with so much bronze that it could not be weighed,

text.NRSV: and cedar logs without number—for the Sidonians and Tyrians brought great quantities of cedar to David.
text.Rotherham: and cedar-trees, even without number,—for the Zidonians and the Tyreans brought cedar-trees in abundance, unto David.
text.KJV: 22:4 Also Cedar trees in abundance: for the Zidonians, and they of Tyre, brought much Cedar wood to Dauid.
text.NABRE: 22:4 and cedar trees without number. The Sidonians and Tyrians brought great stores of cedar logs to David.

text.NRSV: For David said, “My son Solomon is young and inexperienced, and the house that is to be built for the Lord must be exceedingly magnificent, famous and glorified throughout all lands; I will therefore make preparation for it.” So David provided abundantly before his death.
text.Rotherham: And David said, Solomon my son, is young and tender, and, the house to be built, must be great and lofty and famous and beautiful, for all lands,—oh let me then prepare for it. So David prepared abundantly, before his death.
text.KJV: 22:5 And Dauid said, Solomon my sonne is yong and tender, and the house that is to be builded for the Lord, must be exceeding magnificall, of fame and of glory throughout all countreys: I will therefore now make preparation for it. So Dauid prepared abundantly before his death.
text.NABRE: 22:5 David said: “My son Solomon is young and inexperienced; but the house that is to be built for the Lord must be made so magnificent that it will be renowned and glorious in all lands. Therefore I will make preparations for it.” Thus before his death David laid up materials in abundance.

text.NRSV: Then he called for his son Solomon and charged him to build a house for the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Then he called for Solomon his son,—and charged him, to build a house, for Yahweh, God of Israel.
text.KJV: 22:6 ¶ Then hee called for Solomon his sonne, and charged him to build an house for the Lord God of Israel.
text.NABRE: 22:6 Charge to Solomon. Then he summoned his son Solomon and commanded him to build a house for the Lord, the God of Israel.

text.NRSV: David said to Solomon, “My son, I had planned to build a house to the name of the Lord my God.
text.Rotherham: And David said to Solomon his son,—As for me, it was, near my heart, to build a house for the Name of Yahweh my God;
text.KJV: 22:7 And Dauid saide to Solomon; My sonne, as for me, it was in my mind to build an house vnto the Name of the Lord my God.
text.NABRE: 22:7 David said to Solomon: “My son, it was my purpose to build a house myself for the name of the Lord, my God.

text.NRSV: But the word of the Lord came to me, saying, ‘You have shed much blood and have waged great wars; you shall not build a house to my name, because you have shed so much blood in my sight on the earth.
text.Rotherham: but the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying, Blood in abundance, hast thou shed, and, great wars, hast thou made,—thou shalt not build a house for my Name, because, much blood, hast thou shed upon the earth, before me.
text.KJV: 22:8 But the word of the Lord came to mee, saying, Thou hast shed blood abundantly, and hast made great warres: thou shalt not build an house vnto my Name, because thou hast shed much blood vpon the earth in my sight.
text.NABRE: 22:8 But this word of the Lord came to me: You have shed much blood, and you have waged great wars. You may not build a house for my name, because you have shed too much blood upon the earth in my sight.

text.NRSV: See, a son shall be born to you; he shall be a man of peace. I will give him peace from all his enemies on every side; for his name shall be Solomon, and I will give peace and quiet to Israel in his days.
text.Rotherham: Lo! a son, born to thee, He, shall be a man of rest, and I will give him rest from all his enemies, round about,—for, Solomon, shall be his Name, and, peace and quietness, will I bestow upon Israel, in his days;
text.KJV: 22:9 Behold, a sonne shall bee borne to thee, who shall bee a man of rest, and I will giue him rest from all his enemies round about: for his name shalbe Solomon, and I wil giue peace and quietnesse vnto Israel in his dayes.
text.NABRE: 22:9 However, a son will be born to you. He will be a peaceful man, and I will give him rest from all his enemies on every side. For Solomon shall be his name, and in his time I will bestow peace and tranquility on Israel.

text.NRSV: He shall build a house for my name. He shall be a son to me, and I will be a father to him, and I will establish his royal throne in Israel forever.’
text.Rotherham: He, shall build a house for my Name, and, he, shall be my son, and, I, will be his father,—therefore will I establish the throne of his kingdom over Israel, unto times age-abiding:
text.KJV: 22:10 Hee shall build an house for my Name, and he shal be my sonne, and I will be his father, and I will establish the throne of his kingdome ouer Israel for euer.
text.NABRE: 22:10 It is he who shall build a house for my name; he shall be a son to me, and I will be a father to him, and I will establish the throne of his kingship over Israel forever.

text.NRSV: Now, my son, the Lord be with you, so that you may succeed in building the house of the Lord your God, as he has spoken concerning you.
text.Rotherham: Now, my son, Yahweh be with thee,—so shalt thou be prosperous, and shalt build the house of Yahweh thy God, according as he hath spoken concerning thee.
text.KJV: 22:11 Now my sonne, The Lord be with thee, and prosper thou, and build the house of the Lord thy God, as he hath said of thee.
text.NABRE: 22:11 “Now, my son, the Lord be with you, and may you succeed in building the house of the Lord your God, as he has said you shall.

text.NRSV: Only, may the Lord grant you discretion and understanding, so that when he gives you charge over Israel you may keep the law of the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Only Yahweh give thee discretion and understanding, and give thee charge over Israel,—so that thou take heed unto the law of Yahweh thy God.
text.KJV: 22:12 Onely the Lord giue thee wisedome and vnderstanding, and giue thee charge concerning Israel, that thou mayest keepe the Law of the Lord thy God.
text.NABRE: 22:12 But may the Lord give you prudence and discernment when he gives you command over Israel, so that you keep the law of the Lord, your God.

text.NRSV: Then you will prosper if you are careful to observe the statutes and the ordinances that the Lord commanded Moses for Israel. Be strong and of good courage. Do not be afraid or dismayed.
text.Rotherham: Then, shalt thou prosper, if thou take heed to do the statutes and the regulations, which Yahweh commanded Moses, concerning Israel,—be strong and bold, thou mayst not be in fear, nor be dismayed.
text.KJV: 22:13 Then shalt thou prosper, if thou takest heed to fulfill the Statutes and Iudgements which the Lord charged Moses with, concerning Israel: be strong, and of good courage, dread not, nor be dismayed.
text.NABRE: 22:13 Only then shall you succeed, if you are careful to observe the statutes and ordinances which the Lord commanded Moses for Israel. Be strong and steadfast; do not fear or be dismayed.

text.NRSV: With great pains I have provided for the house of the Lord one hundred thousand talents of gold, one million talents of silver, and bronze and iron beyond weighing, for there is so much of it; timber and stone too I have provided. To these you must add more.
text.Rotherham: Lo! then, in my humiliation, have I prepared for the house of Yahweh, of gold a hundred thousand talents, and of silver a thousand thousand talents, and, as for bronze and iron, without weight, for, an abundance, hath it become,—timber also and stone, have I prepared, and, thereunto, thou canst add.
text.KJV: 22:14 Now beholde, in my trouble I haue prepared for the house of the Lord an hundred thousand talents of gold, and a thousand thousand talents of siluer, and of brasse and yron without weight: (for it is in abundance) timber also and stone haue I prepared, and thou mayest adde thereto.
text.NABRE: 22:14 See, with great effort I have laid up for the house of the Lord a hundred thousand talents of gold, a million talents of silver, and bronze and iron in such great quantities that they cannot be weighed. I have also laid up wood and stones, to which you must add.

text.NRSV: You have an abundance of workers: stonecutters, masons, carpenters, and all kinds of artisans without number, skilled in working
text.Rotherham: And, with thee, in abundance, are workmen, hewers and carvers of stone and wood,—and every sort of wise man, in all manner of work:
text.KJV: 22:15 Moreouer, there are workmen with thee in abundance, hewers, and workers of stone and timber, and all maner of cunning men for euery maner of worke:
text.NABRE: 22:15 Moreover, you have available workers, stonecutters, masons, carpenters, and experts in every craft,

text.NRSV: gold, silver, bronze, and iron. Now begin the work, and the Lord be with you.”
text.Rotherham: for gold and for silver and for bronze and for iron, without number. Arise and do, and Yahweh be with thee!
text.KJV: 22:16 Of the gold, the siluer, and the brasse, and the yron, there is no number. Arise therefore, and be doing, and the Lord be with thee.
text.NABRE: 22:16 without number, skilled with gold, silver, bronze, and iron. Set to work, therefore, and the Lord be with you!”

text.NRSV: David also commanded all the leaders of Israel to help his son Solomon, saying,
text.Rotherham: And David charged all the rulers of Israel, to help Solomon his son:—
text.KJV: 22:17 ¶ Dauid also commanded all the Princes of Israel to helpe Solomon his sonne, saying,
text.NABRE: 22:17 Charge to the Officials. David also commanded all of the officials of Israel to help his son Solomon:

text.NRSV: “Is not the Lord your God with you? Has he not given you peace on every side? For he has delivered the inhabitants of the land into my hand; and the land is subdued before the Lord and his people.
text.Rotherham: Is not, Yahweh your God, with you? and hath he not given you rest on every side? for he hath given into my hand, the inhabitants of the land, and subdued is the land before Yahweh, and before his people.
text.KJV: 22:18 Is not the Lord your God with you? and hath he not giuen you rest on euery side? for he hath giuen the inhabitants of the land into mine hand, and the land is subdued before the Lord, and before his people.
text.NABRE: 22:18 “Is not the Lord your God with you? Has he not given you rest on every side? Indeed, he has delivered the inhabitants of the land into my power, and the land is subdued before the Lord and his people.

text.NRSV: Now set your mind and heart to seek the Lord your God. Go and build the sanctuary of the Lord God so that the ark of the covenant of the Lord and the holy vessels of God may be brought into a house built to the name of the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Now, apply your heart and your soul, to seek unto Yahweh your God,—and then arise ye and build the sanctuary of Yahweh Elohim, that ye may bring the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, and the holy vessels of God, into the house which hath been built for the Name of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 22:19 Now set your heart and your soule to seeke the Lord your God: arise therfore, and build ye the Sanctuary of the Lord God, to bring the Arke of the Couenant of the Lord, & the holy vessels of God, into the house that is to be built to the Name of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 22:19 Therefore, devote your hearts and souls to seeking the Lord your God. Proceed to build the sanctuary of the Lord God, that the ark of the covenant of the Lord and God’s sacred vessels may be brought into the house built for the name of the Lord.”

text.NRSV: When David was old and full of days, he made his son Solomon king over Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, David, was old, and satisfied with days,—so he made Solomon his son king, over Israel;
text.KJV: 23:1 So when Dauid was old and full of dayes, he made Solomon his sonne king ouer Israel.
text.NABRE: 23:1 Chapter 23 The Levitical Divisions. When David had grown old and was near the end of his days, he made his son Solomon king over Israel.

text.NRSV: David assembled all the leaders of Israel and the priests and the Levites.
text.Rotherham: and gathered together all the rulers of Israel, with the priests, and the Levites.
text.KJV: 23:2 ¶ And he gathered together all the Princes of Israel, with the Priests and the Leuites.
text.NABRE: 23:2 He then gathered together all the officials of Israel, along with the priests and the Levites.

text.NRSV: The Levites, thirty years old and upward, were counted, and the total of their males was thirty-eight thousand.
text.Rotherham: Now the Levites were numbered, from thirty years old and upwards,—and their number by their polls, of mature men, was thirty-eight thousand.
text.KJV: 23:3 Now the Leuites were numbred from the age of thirtie yeeres and vpward: and their number, by their polles, man by man, was thirtie and eight thousand.
text.NABRE: 23:3 The Levites thirty years old and above were counted, and their total number was found to be thirty-eight thousand.

text.NRSV: “Twenty-four thousand of these,” David said, “shall have charge of the work in the house of the Lord, six thousand shall be officers and judges,
text.Rotherham: Of these—to preside over the work of the house of Yahweh, were twenty-four thousand,—and officers and judges, six thousand;
text.KJV: 23:4 Of which, twentie and foure thousand were to set forward the work of the house of the Lord: and sixe thousand were Officers and Iudges.
text.NABRE: 23:4 Of these, twenty-four thousand were to direct the service of the house of the Lord, six thousand were to be officials and judges,

text.NRSV: four thousand gatekeepers, and four thousand shall offer praises to the Lord with the instruments that I have made for praise.”
text.Rotherham: and four thousand, were doorkeepers,—and, four thousand, were offerers of praise unto Yahweh, with instruments, which [said David] I made for offering praise.
text.KJV: 23:5 Moreouer, foure thousand were porters, and foure thousand praised the Lord with the instruments which I made (said Dauid) to praise therewith.
text.NABRE: 23:5 four thousand were to be gatekeepers, and four thousand were to praise the Lord with the instruments which [David] had devised for praise.

text.NRSV: And David organized them in divisions corresponding to the sons of Levi: Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.
text.Rotherham: And David divided them into courses,—pertaining to the sons of Levi, to Gershon, Kohath and Merari.
text.KJV: 23:6 And Dauid diuided them into courses among the sonnes of Leui, namely Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
text.NABRE: 23:6 David apportioned them into divisions according to the sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

text.NRSV: For the Gershomites were Ladan and Shimei.
text.Rotherham: Of the Gershonites, Ladan and Shimei.
text.KJV: 23:7 ¶ Of the Gershonites were Laadan, and Shimei.
text.NABRE: 23:7 To the Gershonites belonged Ladan and Shimei.

text.NRSV: The sons of Ladan: Jehiel the chief, Zetham, and Joel, three.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Ladan, the chief Jehiel, and Zetham and Joel, three.
text.KJV: 23:8 The sonnes of Laadan, the chiefe was Iehiel, and Zetham, and Ioel, three.
text.NABRE: 23:8 The sons of Ladan: Jehiel the chief, then Zetham and Joel; three in all.

text.NRSV: The sons of Shimei: Shelomoth, Haziel, and Haran, three. These were the heads of families of Ladan.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Shimei, Shelomoth and Haziel and Haran, three,—these, were the ancestral chiefs of Ladan.
text.KJV: 23:9 The sonnes of Shimei: Shelomith, and Haziel, and Haran, three. These were the chiefe of the fathers of Laadan.
text.NABRE: 23:9 The sons of Shimei were Shelomoth, Haziel, and Haran; three. These were the heads of the families of Ladan.

text.NRSV: And the sons of Shimei: Jahath, Zina, Jeush, and Beriah. These four were the sons of Shimei.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Shimei, Jahath, Zina, and Jeush, and Beriah,—these, were the sons of Shimei, four.
text.KJV: 23:10 And the sonnes of Shimei were: Iahath, Zina, and Ieush, and Beriah. These foure were the sonnes of Shimei.
text.NABRE: 23:10 The sons of Shimei were Jahath, Zizah, Jeush, and Beriah; these were the sons of Shimei, four in all.

text.NRSV: Jahath was the chief, and Zizah the second; but Jeush and Beriah did not have many sons, so they were enrolled as a single family.
text.Rotherham: And Jahath was chief, and Zizah the second,—but, Jeush and Beriah, had not many sons, so they became an ancestral house, by one reckoning.
text.KJV: 23:11 And Iahath was the chiefe, and Ziza the second: but Ieush and Beriah had not many sonnes: therefore they were in one reckoning, according to their fathers house.
text.NABRE: 23:11 Jahath was the chief and Zizah was second to him; but Jeush and Beriah had few sons, and therefore they were classed as a single family, exercising a single office.

text.NRSV: The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel, four.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Kohath, Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel, four.
text.KJV: 23:12 ¶ The sonnes of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel, foure.
text.NABRE: 23:12 The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel; four in all.

text.NRSV: The sons of Amram: Aaron and Moses. Aaron was set apart to consecrate the most holy things, he and his sons forever, to offer incense before the Lord, and to minister to him and pronounce blessings in his name forever.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Amram, Aaron and Moses,—and Aaron was separated, to hallow the holy of holies, he and his sons, unto times age-abiding, to make a perfume before Yahweh, to be in attendance upon him, and to bless in his name, unto times age-abiding;
text.KJV: 23:13 The sonnes of Amram: Aaron and Moses: and Aaron was separated, that he should sanctifie the most holy things, he and his sonnes for euer, to burne incense before the Lord, to minister vnto him, and to blesse in his Name for euer.
text.NABRE: 23:13 The sons of Amram were Aaron and Moses. Aaron was set apart to be consecrated as most holy, he and his sons forever, to offer sacrifice before the Lord, to minister to him, and to bless in his name forever.

text.NRSV: But as for Moses the man of God, his sons were to be reckoned among the tribe of Levi.
text.Rotherham: and, as for Moses, the man of God, his sons, were called unto the tribe of Levi.
text.KJV: 23:14 Now concerning Moses the man of God, his sonnes were named of the tribe of Leui.
text.NABRE: 23:14 As for Moses, however, the man of God, his sons were counted as part of the tribe of Levi.

text.NRSV: The sons of Moses: Gershom and Eliezer.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Moses, were Gershom, and Eliezer.
text.KJV: 23:15 The sonnes of Moses were: Gershom and Eliezer.
text.NABRE: 23:15 The sons of Moses were Gershom and Eliezer.

text.NRSV: The son of Gershom: Shebuel the chief.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Gershom, were Shebuel the chief;
text.KJV: 23:16 Of the sonnes of Gershom Shebuel was the chiefe.
text.NABRE: 23:16 The sons of Gershom: Shubael the chief.

text.NRSV: The son of Eliezer: Rehabiah the chief; Eliezer had no other sons, but the sons of Rehabiah were very numerous.
text.Rotherham: and the sons of Eliezer were Rehabiah the chief,—and Eliezer had no other sons, but, the sons of Rehabiah, were very many.
text.KJV: 23:17 And the sonnes of Eliezer were: Rehabiah the chiefe. And Eliezer had none other sonnes: but the sonnes of Rehabiah were very many.
text.NABRE: 23:17 The sons of Eliezer were Rehabiah the chief—Eliezer had no other sons, but the sons of Rehabiah were very numerous.

text.NRSV: The son of Izhar: Shelomith the chief.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Izhar, Shelomith the chief.
text.KJV: 23:18 Of the sonnes of Izhar, Shelomith the chiefe.
text.NABRE: 23:18 The sons of Izhar: Shelomith the chief.

text.NRSV: The sons of Hebron: Jeriah the chief, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, and Jekameam the fourth.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Hebron, Jeriah the chief, Amariah the second, Jehaziel the third, and Jekameam the fourth.
text.KJV: 23:19 Of the sonnes of Hebron, Ieriah the first, Amariah the second, Iahaziel the third, and Iekamiam the fourth.
text.NABRE: 23:19 The sons of Hebron: Jeriah, the chief, Amariah, the second, Jahaziel, the third, and Jekameam, the fourth.

text.NRSV: The sons of Uzziel: Micah the chief and Isshiah the second.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Uzziel, Micah the chief, and Isshiah the second.
text.KJV: 23:20 Of the sonnes of Uzziel: Michah the first, and Iesiah the second.
text.NABRE: 23:20 The sons of Uzziel: Micah, the chief, and Isshiah, the second.

text.NRSV: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. The sons of Mahli: Eleazar and Kish.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Merari, were Mahli and Mushi. The sons of Mahli, were Eleazar and Kish.
text.KJV: 23:21 ¶ The sonnes of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. The sonnes of Mahli: Eleazar and Kish.
text.NABRE: 23:21 The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. The sons of Mahli: Eleazar and Kish.

text.NRSV: Eleazar died having no sons, but only daughters; their kindred, the sons of Kish, married them.
text.Rotherham: But Eleazar died, and had no sons, but daughters only,—and so the sons of Kish their brethren took them.
text.KJV: 23:22 And Eleazar died, and had no sonnes but daughters: and their brethren the sonnes of Kish tooke them.
text.NABRE: 23:22 Eleazar died leaving no sons, only daughters; the sons of Kish, their kinsmen, married them.

text.NRSV: The sons of Mushi: Mahli, Eder, and Jeremoth, three.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Mushi, were Mahli and Eder and Jeremoth, three.
text.KJV: 23:23 The sonnes of Mushi: Mahli, and Eder, and Ierimoth, three.
text.NABRE: 23:23 The sons of Mushi: Mahli, Eder, and Jeremoth; three in all.

text.NRSV: These were the sons of Levi by their ancestral houses, the heads of families as they were enrolled according to the number of the names of the individuals from twenty years old and upward who were to do the work for the service of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: These, were the sons of Levi by their ancestral house, the ancestral chiefs of them who were counted in the number of their names, by their polls, doing the work, for the service of the house of Yahweh,—from twenty years old, and upwards.
text.KJV: 23:24 ¶ These were the sonnes of Leui after the house of their fathers, euen the chiefe of the fathers, as they were counted by number of names by their polles, that did the worke for the seruice of the house of the Lord, from the age of twentie yeeres and vpward.
text.NABRE: 23:24 These were the sons of Levi according to their ancestral houses, the family heads as they were enrolled one by one according to their names. They performed the work of the service of the house of the Lord beginning at twenty years of age.

text.NRSV: For David said, “The Lord, the God of Israel, has given rest to his people; and he dwells in Jerusalem forever.
text.Rotherham: For, said David, Yahweh the God of Israel hath given rest unto his people,—and hath taken up his habitation in Jerusalem, unto times age-abiding;
text.KJV: 23:25 For Dauid sayd, The Lord God of Israel hath giuen rest vnto his people, that they may dwell in Ierusalem for euer.
text.NABRE: 23:25 David said: “The Lord, the God of Israel, has given rest to his people, and has taken up his dwelling in Jerusalem forever.

text.NRSV: And so the Levites no longer need to carry the tabernacle or any of the vessels for its service.”
text.Rotherham: moreover also, as for the Levites, they have no need to bear the habitation, nor any of its vessels in the laborious work thereof.
text.KJV: 23:26 And also vnto the Leuites: they shall no more cary the Tabernacle, nor any vessels of it for the seruice thereof.
text.NABRE: 23:26 Henceforth the Levites need not carry the tabernacle or any of the equipment for its service.”

text.NRSV: For according to the last words of David, the sons of Levi were numbered from twenty years old and upward.
text.Rotherham: For, by the last words of David, the same, were the number of the sons of Levi,—from twenty years old, and upwards.
text.KJV: 23:27 For by the last words of Dauid, the Leuites were numbred from twentie yeeres olde, and aboue:
text.NABRE: 23:27 For by David’s last words the Levites were enlisted from the time they were twenty years old.

text.NRSV: For their duty was to assist the sons of Aaron in the service of the house of the Lord, having the care of the courts and the chambers, the cleansing of all that is holy, and any work for the service of the house of God;
text.Rotherham: For, their duty, was—to be in attendance upon the sons of Aaron, in the laborious work of the house of Yahweh, over the courts, and over the chambers, and over the purifying of everything holy,—and doing the laborious work, of the house of God;
text.KJV: 23:28 Because their office was to wait on the sonnes of Aaron, for the seruice of the house of the Lord, in the courts, and in the chambers, and in the purifying of all holy things, and the worke of the seruice of the house of God:
text.NABRE: 23:28 Their duty is to assist the sons of Aaron in the service of the house of the Lord, having charge of the courts, the chambers, and the preservation of everything holy: they take part in the service of the house of God.

text.NRSV: to assist also with the rows of bread, the choice flour for the grain offering, the wafers of unleavened bread, the baked offering, the offering mixed with oil, and all measures of quantity or size;
text.Rotherham: and for the bread set in array, and for the fine meal for the meal-offering, and for the unleavened cakes, even for that which is baked in a pan, for the moistened food,—even of every measure, and size;
text.KJV: 23:29 Both for the shew-bread, and for the fine floure for meat offering, and for the vnleauened cakes, and for that which is baked in the panne, and for that which is fried, and for all maner of measures and size:
text.NABRE: 23:29 They also have charge of the showbread, of the fine flour for the grain offering, of the wafers of unleavened bread, and of the baking and mixing, and of all measures of quantity and size.

text.NRSV: and to stand every morning, thanking and praising the Lord, and likewise at evening,
text.Rotherham: and to stand, morning by morning, to give thanks and offer praise unto Yahweh,—and, likewise, at even;
text.KJV: 23:30 And to stand euery morning to thanke and praise the Lord, and likewise at Euen:
text.NABRE: 23:30 They are to be present every morning to offer thanks and to praise the Lord, and likewise in the evening;

text.NRSV: and whenever burnt offerings are offered to the Lord on sabbaths, new moons, and appointed festivals, according to the number required of them, regularly before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and for all the offering of ascending-sacrifices unto Yahweh, on the sabbaths, and on the new moons, and on the set feasts,—by number, according to the manner prescribed for them continually, before Yahweh;—
text.KJV: 23:31 And to offer all burnt sacrifices vnto the Lord in the Sabbaths, in the new moones, and on the set feasts, by number, according to the order commanded vnto them continually before the Lord:
text.NABRE: 23:31 and at every sacrifice of burnt offerings to the Lord on sabbaths, new moons, and feast days, in such numbers as are prescribed, they must always be present before the Lord

text.NRSV: Thus they shall keep charge of the tent of meeting and the sanctuary, and shall attend the sons of Aaron, their kindred, for the service of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and they shall keep the charge of the tent of meeting, and the charge of the holy place, and the charge of the sons of Aaron, their brethren,—in the laborious work of the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 23:32 And that they should keepe the charge of the Tabernacle of the Congregation, and the charge of the holy place, and the charge of the sonnes of Aaron their brethren, in the seruice of the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 23:32 and observe what is prescribed for them concerning the tent of meeting, the sanctuary, and the sons of Aaron, their kinsmen, in the service of the house of the Lord.

text.NRSV: The divisions of the descendants of Aaron were these. The sons of Aaron: Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
text.Rotherham: Now, the sons of Aaron, had their courses,—the sons of Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar;
text.KJV: 24:1 Now these are the diuisions of the sonnes of Aaron. The sonnes of Aaron: Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.
text.NABRE: 24:1 Chapter 24 The Priestly Divisions. There were also divisions for the sons of Aaron. The sons of Aaron were Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

text.NRSV: But Nadab and Abihu died before their father, and had no sons; so Eleazar and Ithamar became the priests.
text.Rotherham: but Nadab and Abihu, died, before their father, and sons, had they none,—but Eleazar and Ithamar became priests;
text.KJV: 24:2 But Nadab and Abihu died before their father, and had no children: Therefore Eleazar and Ithamar executed the Priests office.
text.NABRE: 24:2 Nadab and Abihu died before their father, leaving no sons; therefore only Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests.

text.NRSV: Along with Zadok of the sons of Eleazar, and Ahimelech of the sons of Ithamar, David organized them according to the appointed duties in their service.
text.Rotherham: and David apportioned to them courses, both to Zadok of the sons of Eleazar, and to Ahimelech of the sons of Ithamar,—by their appointed place in their service.
text.KJV: 24:3 And Dauid distributed them, both Zadok of the sonnes of Eleazar, and Ahimelech of the sonnes of Ithamar, according to their offices in their seruice.
text.NABRE: 24:3 David, with Zadok, a descendant of Eleazar, and Ahimelech, a descendant of Ithamar, apportioned them their offices in the priestly service.

text.NRSV: Since more chief men were found among the sons of Eleazar than among the sons of Ithamar, they organized them under sixteen heads of ancestral houses of the sons of Eleazar, and eight of the sons of Ithamar.
text.Rotherham: But the sons of Eleazar were found to be more numerous, by the chiefs of their able men, than were the sons of Ithamar, so they divided them,—the sons of Eleazar, had chiefs, of the ancestral house, sixteen, whereas, the sons of Ithamar, had of their ancestral house, eight.
text.KJV: 24:4 And there were moe chiefe men found of the sonnes of Eleazar, then of the sonnes of Ithamar: and thus were they diuided. Among the sonnes of Eleazar there were sixteene chiefe men of the house of their fathers, and eight among the sonnes of Ithamar according to the house of their fathers.
text.NABRE: 24:4 But since the sons of Eleazar were found to be more numerous by male heads than those of Ithamar, the former were divided into sixteen groups, and the latter into eight groups, each under its family heads.

text.NRSV: They organized them by lot, all alike, for there were officers of the sanctuary and officers of God among both the sons of Eleazar and the sons of Ithamar.
text.Rotherham: They divided them, therefore, by [casting] lots, these with those,—for there were princes of the sanctuary, and princes of God, from among the sons of Eleazar, and among the sons of Ithamar.
text.KJV: 24:5 Thus were they diuided by lot, one sort with another; for the gouernours of the Sanctuarie, and gouernours of the house of God, were of the sonnes of Eleazar, and of the sonnes of Ithamar.
text.NABRE: 24:5 Their functions were assigned impartially by lot, for there were officers of the holy place, and officers of God, descended both from Eleazar and from Ithamar.

text.NRSV: The scribe Shemaiah son of Nethanel, a Levite, recorded them in the presence of the king, and the officers, and Zadok the priest, and Ahimelech son of Abiathar, and the heads of ancestral houses of the priests and of the Levites; one ancestral house being chosen for Eleazar and one chosen for Ithamar.
text.Rotherham: And Shemaiah son of Nethanel the scribe from among the Levites, wrote them down, before the king and the rulers and Zadok the priest, and Ahimelech son of Abiathar, and the ancestral chiefs, pertaining to the priests and to the Levites,—one ancestral house, was taken for Eleazar, and, was equally taken, for Ithamar.
text.KJV: 24:6 And Shemaiah the sonne of Nathanael the Scribe, one of the Leuites, wrote them before the King and the Princes, and Zadok the Priest, and Ahimelech the sonne of Abiathar, and before the chiefe of the fathers of the priests and Leuites: one principall houshold being taken for Eleazar, and one taken for Ithamar.
text.NABRE: 24:6 The scribe Shemaiah, son of Nethanel, a Levite, recorded them in the presence of the king, and of the officials, of Zadok the priest, and of Ahimelech, son of Abiathar, and of the heads of the ancestral houses of the priests and of the Levites, listing two successive family groups from Eleazar before each one from Ithamar.

text.NRSV: The first lot fell to Jehoiarib, the second to Jedaiah,
text.Rotherham: So then the first lot came forth for Jehoiarib, for Jedaiah, the second;
text.KJV: 24:7 Now the first lot came foorth to Iehoiarib: the second to Iedaiah,
text.NABRE: 24:7 The first lot fell to Jehoiarib, the second to Jedaiah,

text.NRSV: the third to Harim, the fourth to Seorim,
text.Rotherham: for Harim, the third, for Seorim, the fourth;
text.KJV: 24:8 The third to Harim, the fourth to Seorim,
text.NABRE: 24:8 the third to Harim, the fourth to Seorim,

text.NRSV: the fifth to Malchijah, the sixth to Mijamin,
text.Rotherham: for Malchijah, the fifth, for Mijamin, the sixth;
text.KJV: 24:9 The fifth to Malchiiah, the sixth to Miiamin,
text.NABRE: 24:9 the fifth to Malchijah, the sixth to Mijamin,

text.NRSV: the seventh to Hakkoz, the eighth to Abijah,
text.Rotherham: for Hakkoz, the seventh, for Abijah, the eighth;
text.KJV: 24:10 The seuenth to Hakkoz, the eight to Abiiah,
text.NABRE: 24:10 the seventh to Hakkoz, the eighth to Abijah,

text.NRSV: the ninth to Jeshua, the tenth to Shecaniah,
text.Rotherham: for Jeshua, the ninth, for Shecaniah, the tenth;
text.KJV: 24:11 The ninth to Ieshua, the tenth to Shecaniah,
text.NABRE: 24:11 the ninth to Jeshua, the tenth to Shecaniah,

text.NRSV: the eleventh to Eliashib, the twelfth to Jakim,
text.Rotherham: for Eliashib, the eleventh, for Jakim, the twelfth;
text.KJV: 24:12 The eleuenth to Eliashib, the twelfth to Iakim,
text.NABRE: 24:12 the eleventh to Eliashib, the twelfth to Jakim,

text.NRSV: the thirteenth to Huppah, the fourteenth to Jeshebeab,
text.Rotherham: for Huppah, the thirteenth, for Jeshebeab, the fourteenth;
text.KJV: 24:13 The thirteenth to Huppah, the fourteenth to Ieshebeab,
text.NABRE: 24:13 the thirteenth to Huppah, the fourteenth to Ishbaal,

text.NRSV: the fifteenth to Bilgah, the sixteenth to Immer,
text.Rotherham: for Bilgah, the fifteenth, for Immer, the sixteenth;
text.KJV: 24:14 The fifteenth to Bilgah, the sixteenth to Immer,
text.NABRE: 24:14 the fifteenth to Bilgah, the sixteenth to Immer,

text.NRSV: the seventeenth to Hezir, the eighteenth to Happizzez,
text.Rotherham: for Hezir, the seventeenth, for Happizzez, the eighteenth;
text.KJV: 24:15 The seuenteenth to Hezir, the eighteenth to Aphses,
text.NABRE: 24:15 the seventeenth to Hezir, the eighteenth to Happizzez,

text.NRSV: the nineteenth to Pethahiah, the twentieth to Jehezkel,
text.Rotherham: for Pethahiah, the nineteenth, for Jehezkel, the twentieth;
text.KJV: 24:16 The ninteenth to Pethahiah, the twentieth to Iehezekel,
text.NABRE: 24:16 the nineteenth to Pethahiah, the twentieth to Jehezkel,

text.NRSV: the twenty-first to Jachin, the twenty-second to Gamul,
text.Rotherham: for Jachin, the twenty-first, for Gamul, the twenty-second;
text.KJV: 24:17 The one and twentieth to Iachin, the two and twentieth to Gamul,
text.NABRE: 24:17 the twenty-first to Jachin, the twenty-second to Gamul,

text.NRSV: the twenty-third to Delaiah, the twenty-fourth to Maaziah.
text.Rotherham: for Delaiah, the twenty-third, for Maaziah, the twenty-fourth.
text.KJV: 24:18 The three and twentieth to Delaiah, the foure and twentieth to Maaziah.
text.NABRE: 24:18 the twenty-third to Delaiah, the twenty-fourth to Maaziah.

text.NRSV: These had as their appointed duty in their service to enter the house of the Lord according to the procedure established for them by their ancestor Aaron, as the Lord God of Israel had commanded him.
text.Rotherham: These, were their appointed places for their service, for entering the house of Yahweh, according to the regulation of them, by the hand of Aaron their father,—just as Yahweh God of Israel, commanded him.
text.KJV: 24:19 These were the orderings of them in their seruice to come into the house of the Lord according to their maner, vnder Aaron their father, as the Lord God of Israel had commanded him.
text.NABRE: 24:19 This was the appointed order of their service when they functioned in the house of the Lord according to the precepts given them by Aaron, their father, as the Lord, the God of Israel, had commanded him.

text.NRSV: And of the rest of the sons of Levi: of the sons of Amram, Shubael; of the sons of Shubael, Jehdeiah.
text.Rotherham: Now, as for the sons of Levi who remained, of the sons of Amram, Shubael, of the sons of Shubael, Jehdeiah.
text.KJV: 24:20 ¶ And the rest of the sonnes of Leui were these: of the sons of Amram, Shubael: of the sonnes of Shubael, Iedeiah.
text.NABRE: 24:20 Other Levites. Of the remaining Levites, there were Shubael, of the sons of Amram, and Jehdeiah, of the sons of Shubael;

text.NRSV: Of Rehabiah: of the sons of Rehabiah, Isshiah the chief.
text.Rotherham: Of Rehabiah, of the sons of Rehabiah, the chief Isshiah;
text.KJV: 24:21 Concerning Rehabiah, of the sons of Rehabiah, the first was Isshiah.
text.NABRE: 24:21 Isshiah, the chief, of the sons of Rehabiah;

text.NRSV: Of the Izharites, Shelomoth; of the sons of Shelomoth, Jahath.
text.Rotherham: of the Izharites, Shelomoth,—of the sons of Shelomoth, Jahath;
text.KJV: 24:22 Of the Izharites, Shelomoth: of the sonnes of Shelomoth, Iahath.
text.NABRE: 24:22 Shelomith of the Izharites, and Jahath of the sons of Shelomith.

text.NRSV: The sons of Hebron: Jeriah the chief, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, Jekameam the fourth.
text.Rotherham: and, the sons [of Hebron], Jeriah,—Amariah, the second, Jehaziel, the third, Jekameam, the fourth;
text.KJV: 24:23 And the sonnes of Hebron, Ieriah the first, Amariah the second, Iahaziel the third, Iekameam the fourth.
text.NABRE: 24:23 The sons of Hebron were Jeriah, the chief, Amariah, the second, Jahaziel, the third, Jekameam, the fourth.

text.NRSV: The son of Uzziel, Micah; of the sons of Micah, Shamir.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Uzziel, Micah, of the sons of Micah, Shamir;
text.KJV: 24:24 Of the sonnes of Uzziel, Michah: of the sonnes of Michah, Shamir.
text.NABRE: 24:24 The sons of Uzziel were Micah; Shamir, of the sons of Micah;

text.NRSV: The brother of Micah, Isshiah; of the sons of Isshiah, Zechariah.
text.Rotherham: the brother of Micah, Isshiah, of the sons of Isshiah, Zechariah.
text.KJV: 24:25 The brother of Michah was Isshiah: of the sonnes of Isshiah, Zechariah.
text.NABRE: 24:25 Isshiah, the brother of Micah; and Zechariah, a descendant of Isshiah.

text.NRSV: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. The son of Jaaziah: Beno.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Merari, Mahli and Mushi,—the sons of Jaaziah, Beno;
text.KJV: 24:26 The sonnes of Merari were Mahli and Mushi: the sonnes of Iaaziah, Beno.
text.NABRE: 24:26 The sons of Merari were Mahli, Mushi, and the sons of his son Uzziah.

text.NRSV: The sons of Merari: of Jaaziah, Beno, Shoham, Zaccur, and Ibri.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Merari, of Jaaziah, Beno, and Shoham and Zaccur and Ibri:
text.KJV: 24:27 ¶ The sonnes of Merari by Iaaziah, Beno, and Shoham, and Zaccur, and Ibri.
text.NABRE: 24:27 The sons of Merari through his son Uzziah: Shoham, Zaccur, and Ibri.

text.NRSV: Of Mahli: Eleazar, who had no sons.
text.Rotherham: of Mahli, Eleazar, who had no sons;
text.KJV: 24:28 Of Mahli came Eleazar, who had no sonnes.
text.NABRE: 24:28 The sons of Mahli were Eleazar, who had no sons,

text.NRSV: Of Kish, the son of Kish: Jerahmeel.
text.Rotherham: of Kish—the sons of Kish, Jerameel;
text.KJV: 24:29 Concerning Kish: the sonne of Kish was Ierahmeel.
text.NABRE: 24:29 and Jerahmeel, of the sons of Kish.

text.NRSV: The sons of Mushi: Mahli, Eder, and Jerimoth. These were the sons of the Levites according to their ancestral houses.
text.Rotherham: and, the sons of Mushi, Mahli and Eder and Jerimoth. These, were the sons of the Levites, belonging to their ancestral house.
text.KJV: 24:30 The sonnes also of Mushi, Mahli, and Eder, and Ierimoth. These were the sonnes of the Leuites after the house of their fathers.
text.NABRE: 24:30 The sons of Mushi were Mahli, Eder, and Jerimoth. These were the sons of the Levites according to their ancestral houses.

text.NRSV: These also cast lots just as their kindred the sons of Aaron did in the presence of King David, Zadok, Ahimelech, and the heads of ancestral houses of the priests and of the Levites, the chief as well as the youngest brother, casting lots alike.
