text.Rotherham: Moreover, they also, cast lots along with their brethren the sons of Aaron, before David the king and Zadok and Ahimelech, and the ancestral chiefs, pertaining to the priests and to the Levites, the ancestral chief along with his younger brethren.
text.KJV: 24:31 These likewise cast lots ouer against their brethren the sonnes of Aaron, in the presence of Dauid the King, and Zadok and Ahimelech, and the chiefe of the fathers of the priests and Leuites, euen the principall fathers ouer against their yonger brethren.
text.NABRE: 24:31 They too, in the same manner as their kinsmen, the sons of Aaron, cast lots in the presence of King David, Zadok, Ahimelech, and the heads of the priestly and levitical families; the more important family did so in the same way as the less important one.

text.NRSV: David and the officers of the army also set apart for the service the sons of Asaph, and of Heman, and of Jeduthun, who should prophesy with lyres, harps, and cymbals. The list of those who did the work and of their duties was:
text.Rotherham: And David and the captains of the host set apart for service, unto the sons of Asaph and Heman and Jeduthun, such as should prophesy, with lyres, with harps, and with cymbals,—and, the number of the workers, for their service, was:
text.KJV: 25:1 Moreouer Dauid and the captaines of the hoste separated to the seruice of the sonnes of Asaph, and of Heman, and of Ieduthun, who should prophesie with harps, with psalteries, and with cymbals: and the number of the workmen, according to their seruice, was:
text.NABRE: 25:1 Chapter 25 The Singers. David and the leaders of the liturgy set apart for the service the sons of Asaph, Heman, and Jeduthun, who prophesied to the accompaniment of lyres and harps and cymbals. This is the list of those who performed this service:

text.NRSV: Of the sons of Asaph: Zaccur, Joseph, Nethaniah, and Asarelah, sons of Asaph, under the direction of Asaph, who prophesied under the direction of the king.
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Asaph, Zaccur and Joseph and Nethaniah and Asarelah, the sons of Asaph,—under the direction of Asaph, who prophesied under the direction of the king.
text.KJV: 25:2 Of the sonnes of Asaph: Zaccur, and Ioseph, and Nethaniah, and Asarelah, the sonnes of Asaph vnder the hands of Asaph, which prophesied according to the order of the king.
text.NABRE: 25:2 Of the sons of Asaph: Zaccur, Joseph, Nethaniah, and Asharelah, sons of Asaph, under the direction of Asaph, who prophesied under the guidance of the king.

text.NRSV: Of Jeduthun, the sons of Jeduthun: Gedaliah, Zeri, Jeshaiah, Shimei, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah, six, under the direction of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied with the lyre in thanksgiving and praise to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Of Jeduthun, the sons of Jeduthun,—Gedaliah, and Zeri, and Jeshaiah, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah—six, under the direction of their father Jeduthun, with the lyre, as he prophesied, in giving thanks and offering praise unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 25:3 Of Ieduthun: the sonnes of Ieduthun, Gedaliah, and Zeri, and Ieshaiah, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah, sixe, vnder the handes of their father Ieduthun, who prophesied with a harpe, to giue thankes and to praise the Lord.
text.NABRE: 25:3 Of Jeduthun, these sons of Jeduthun: Gedaliah, Zeri, Jeshaiah, Shimei, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah; six, under the direction of their father Jeduthun, who prophesied to the accompaniment of a lyre, to give thanks and praise to the Lord.

text.NRSV: Of Heman, the sons of Heman: Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, and Jerimoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, and Romamti-ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, Mahazioth.
text.Rotherham: Of Heman, the sons of Heman—Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, and Jeremoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, and Romamti-ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, Mahazioth.
text.KJV: 25:4 Of Heman: the sonnes of Heman, Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, and Ierimoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliatha, Giddalti, and Romamti-Ezer, Ioshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, and Mahazioth:
text.NABRE: 25:4 Of Heman, these sons of Heman: Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shubael, and Jerimoth; Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, Romamti-ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, and Mahazioth.

text.NRSV: All these were the sons of Heman the king’s seer, according to the promise of God to exalt him; for God had given Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.
text.Rotherham: All these, were sons of Heman, the seer of the king in the things of God, at the lifting of the horn. And God gave to Heman, fourteen sons and three daughters,
text.KJV: 25:5 All these were the sonnes of Heman the kings Seer in the wordes of God, to lift vp the horne. And God gaue to Heman fourteene sonnes and three daughters.
text.NABRE: 25:5 All these were the sons of Heman, the king’s seer for divine matters; to exalt him God gave Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.

text.NRSV: They were all under the direction of their father for the music in the house of the Lord with cymbals, harps, and lyres for the service of the house of God. Asaph, Jeduthun, and Heman were under the order of the king.
text.Rotherham: All these, were under the direction of their father, in the singing of the house of Yahweh, with cymbals, harps and lyres, for the service of the house of God,—Asaph and Jeduthun and Heman, under the direction of the king.
text.KJV: 25:6 All these were vnder the hands of their father, for song in the house of the Lord with cymbals, psalteries and harpes, for the seruice of the house of God, according to the kings order, to Asaph, Ieduthun, and Heman.
text.NABRE: 25:6 All these, whether of Asaph, Jeduthun, or Heman, were under their fathers’ direction in the singing in the house of the Lord to the accompaniment of cymbals, harps and lyres, serving in the house of God, under the guidance of the king.

text.NRSV: The number of them along with their kindred, who were trained in singing to the Lord, all of whom were skillful, was two hundred eighty-eight.
text.Rotherham: And, the number of them—with their brethren, who were instructed in singing unto Yahweh,—even all the skilful, was two hundred and eighty-eight.
text.KJV: 25:7 So the number of them, with their brethren that were instructed in the songs of the Lord, euen all that were cunning, was two hundred, foure score and eight.
text.NABRE: 25:7 Their number, together with that of their kinsmen who were trained in singing to the Lord, all of them skilled men, was two hundred and eighty-eight.

text.NRSV: And they cast lots for their duties, small and great, teacher and pupil alike.
text.Rotherham: So they cast lots for their charges, all alike, as the small so the great, the teacher with the learner.
text.KJV: 25:8 ¶ And they cast lots ward against ward, as well the small as the great, the teacher as the scholler.
text.NABRE: 25:8 They cast lots for their functions equally, young and old, master and pupil alike.

text.NRSV: The first lot fell for Asaph to Joseph; the second to Gedaliah, to him and his kindred and his sons, twelve;
text.Rotherham: And the first lot came forth for Asaph, to Joseph,—Gedaliah, the second, he and his brethren and his sons, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:9 Now the first lot came foorth for Asaph to Ioseph, the second to Gedaliah, who with his brethren and sonnes were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:9 The first lot fell to Asaph, to the family of Joseph; he and his sons and his kinsmen were twelve. Gedaliah was the second; he and his kinsmen and his sons were twelve.

text.NRSV: the third to Zaccur, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The third, Zaccur, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:10 The third to Zaccur, he, his sons, and his brethren were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:10 The third was Zaccur, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the fourth to Izri, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The fourth, to Izri, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:11 The fourth to Izri, he, his sonnes and his brethren were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:11 The fourth fell to Izri, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the fifth to Nethaniah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The fifth, Nethaniah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:12 The fift to Nethaniah, hee, his sonnes and his brethren were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:12 The fifth was Nethaniah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the sixth to Bukkiah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The sixth, Bukkiah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:13 The sixt to Bukkiah, he, his sons and his brethren were twelue.
text.NABRE: 25:13 The sixth was Bukkiah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the seventh to Jesarelah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The seventh, Jesharelah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:14 The seuenth to Iesharelah, hee, his sonnes & his brethren were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:14 The seventh was Jesarelah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the eighth to Jeshaiah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The eighth, Jeshaiah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:15 The eight to Ieshaiah, hee, his sonnes and his brethren, were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:15 The eighth was Jeshaiah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the ninth to Mattaniah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The ninth, Mattaniah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:16 The ninth to Mattaniah, he, his sonnes and his brethren were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:16 The ninth was Mattaniah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the tenth to Shimei, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The tenth, Shimei, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:17 The tenth to Shimei, he, his sons and his brethren were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:17 The tenth was Shimei, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the eleventh to Azarel, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The eleventh, Azarel, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:18 The eleuenth to Azareel, hee, his sonnes and his brethren were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:18 The eleventh was Uzziel, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the twelfth to Hashabiah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: The twelfth, to Hashabiah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:19 The twelfth to Hashabiah, he, his sonnes and his brethren, were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:19 The twelfth fell to Hashabiah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the thirteenth to Shubael, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the thirteenth, Shubael, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:20 The thirteenth to Shubael, hee, his sonnes and his brethren were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:20 The thirteenth was Shubael, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the fourteenth to Mattithiah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the fourteenth, Mattithiah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:21 The fourteenth to Mattithiah, hee, his sonnes and his brethren, were twelue.
text.NABRE: 25:21 The fourteenth was Mattithiah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the fifteenth to Jeremoth, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the fifteenth, to Jeremoth, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:22 The fifteenth to Ierimoth, hee, his sonnes & his brethren, were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:22 The fifteenth fell to Jeremoth, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the sixteenth to Hananiah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the sixteenth, to Hananiah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:23 The sixteenth to Hananiah, hee, his sonnes & his brethren, were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:23 The sixteenth fell to Hananiah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the seventeenth to Joshbekashah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the seventeenth, to Joshbekashah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:24 The seuenteenth to Ioshbekashah, he, his sonnes and his brethren, were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:24 The seventeenth fell to Joshbekashah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the eighteenth to Hanani, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the eighteenth, to Hanani, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:25 The eighteenth to Hanani: hee, his sonnes & his brethren, were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:25 The eighteenth fell to Hanani, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the nineteenth to Mallothi, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the nineteenth, to Mallothi, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:26 The nineteenth to Mallothi, hee, his sonnes & his brethren, were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:26 The nineteenth fell to Mallothi, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the twentieth to Eliathah, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the twentieth, to Eliathah, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:27 The twentieth to Eliathah, hee, his sonnes & his brethren, were twelue:
text.NABRE: 25:27 The twentieth fell to Eliathah, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the twenty-first to Hothir, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the twenty-first, to Hothir, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:28 The one and twentieth to Hothir, he, his sonnes and his brethren were twelue.
text.NABRE: 25:28 The twenty-first fell to Hothir, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the twenty-second to Giddalti, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the twenty-second, to Giddalti, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:29 The two and twentieth to Giddalti, hee, his sonnes and his brethren, were twelue.
text.NABRE: 25:29 The twenty-second fell to Giddalti, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the twenty-third to Mahazioth, his sons and his kindred, twelve;
text.Rotherham: As the twenty-third, to Mahazioth, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:30 The three and twentieth to Mahazioth, he, his sonnes and his brethren, were twelue.
text.NABRE: 25:30 The twenty-third fell to Mahazioth, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: the twenty-fourth to Romamti-ezer, his sons and his kindred, twelve.
text.Rotherham: As the twenty-fourth, to Romamti-ezer, his sons and his brethren, twelve.
text.KJV: 25:31 The foure and twentieth to Romamti-Ezer, he, his sonnes and his brethren, were twelue.
text.NABRE: 25:31 The twenty-fourth fell to Romamti-ezer, his sons, and his kinsmen: twelve.

text.NRSV: As for the divisions of the gatekeepers: of the Korahites, Meshelemiah son of Kore, of the sons of Asaph.
text.Rotherham: To the courses of the door-keepers, pertained,—of the Korahites, Meshelemiah son of Kore, of the sons of Asaph;
text.KJV: 26:1 Concerning the diuisions of the porters: of the Korhites was Meshelemiah the sonne of Kore, of the sonnes of Asaph.
text.NABRE: 26:1 Chapter 26 Divisions of Gatekeepers. As for the divisions of gatekeepers: Of the Korahites was Meshelemiah, the son of Kore, one of the sons of Abiasaph.

text.NRSV: Meshelemiah had sons: Zechariah the firstborn, Jediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Jathniel the fourth,
text.Rotherham: and, Meshelemiah, had sons,—Zechariah the firstborn, Jediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Jathniel the fourth;
text.KJV: 26:2 And the sonnes of Meshelemiah were Zechariah the first borne, Iediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Iathniel the fourth,
text.NABRE: 26:2 Meshelemiah’s sons: Zechariah, the firstborn, Jediael, the second son, Zebadiah, the third, Jathniel, the fourth,

text.NRSV: Elam the fifth, Jehohanan the sixth, Eliehoenai the seventh.
text.Rotherham: Elam the fifth, Jehohanan the sixth, Eliehoenai the seventh.
text.KJV: 26:3 Elam the fifth, Iehohanan the sixth, Elioenai the seuenth.
text.NABRE: 26:3 Elam, the fifth, Jehohanan, the sixth, Eliehoenai, the seventh.

text.NRSV: Obed-edom had sons: Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Joah the third, Sachar the fourth, Nethanel the fifth,
text.Rotherham: And, Obed-edom, had sons,—Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Josh the third, and Sacar the fourth, and Nethanel the fifth;
text.KJV: 26:4 Moreouer the sonnes of Obed-Edom were Shemaiah the first borne, Iehozabad the second, Ioah the thirde, and Sacar the fourth, and Nethaneel the fifth.
text.NABRE: 26:4 Obed-edom’s sons: Shemaiah, the firstborn, Jehozabad, a second son, Joah, the third, Sachar, the fourth, Nethanel, the fifth,

text.NRSV: Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, Peullethai the eighth; for God blessed him.
text.Rotherham: Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, Peullethai, the eighth,—for God had blessed him.
text.KJV: 26:5 Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seuenth, Peulthai the eighth: for God blessed him.
text.NABRE: 26:5 Ammiel, the sixth, Issachar, the seventh, Peullethai, the eighth, for God blessed him.

text.NRSV: Also to his son Shemaiah were sons born who exercised authority in their ancestral houses, for they were men of great ability.
text.Rotherham: And, to Shemaiah his son, were born sons, who bare rule to their ancestral house,—for, heroes of valour, were they.
text.KJV: 26:6 Also vnto Shemaiah his sonne were sonnes borne, that ruled throughout the house of their father: for they were mighty men of valour.
text.NABRE: 26:6 To his son Shemaiah were born sons who ruled over their family, for they were warriors.

text.NRSV: The sons of Shemaiah: Othni, Rephael, Obed, and Elzabad, whose brothers were able men, Elihu and Semachiah.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Shemaiah, were Othni and Rephael, and Obed, Elzabad, whose brethren were sons of valour,—Elihu, and Semachiah.
text.KJV: 26:7 The sonnes of Shemaiah: Othni, and Rephael, and Obed, Elzabad, whose brethren were strong men; Elihu, and Semachiah.
text.NABRE: 26:7 The sons of Shemaiah were Othni, Rephael, Obed, and Elzabad; also his kinsmen who were men of substance, Elihu and Semachiah.

text.NRSV: All these were of the sons of Obed-edom with their sons and kindred, able men qualified for the service; sixty-two of Obed-edom.
text.Rotherham: All these, were of the sons of Obed-edom, they and their sons and their brethren, able men in strength for the service,—sixty-two, pertaining to Obed-edom.
text.KJV: 26:8 All these of the sonnes of Obed-Edom: they and their sonnes and their brethren, able men for strength for the seruice, were threescore and two of Obed-Edom.
text.NABRE: 26:8 All these were the sons of Obed-edom, who, together with their sons and their kinsmen, were men of substance, fit for the service. Of Obed-edom, sixty-two.

text.NRSV: Meshelemiah had sons and kindred, able men, eighteen.
text.Rotherham: And, Meshelemiah, had sons, and brethren, sons of valour, eighteen.
text.KJV: 26:9 And Meshelemiah had sonnes and brethren, strong men, eighteene.
text.NABRE: 26:9 Of Meshelemiah, eighteen sons and kinsmen, men of substance.

text.NRSV: Hosah, of the sons of Merari, had sons: Shimri the chief (for though he was not the firstborn, his father made him chief),
text.Rotherham: And, Hosah of the sons of Merari, had sons,—Shimri the chief, for though he was not the firstborn, yet his father, made him, chief.
text.KJV: 26:10 Also Hosah of the children of Merari, had sonnes: Simri the chiefe, (for though he was not the first borne, yet his father made him the chiefe)
text.NABRE: 26:10 Hosah, a descendant of Merari, had these sons: Shimri, the chief (for though he was not the firstborn, his father made him chief),

text.NRSV: Hilkiah the second, Tebaliah the third, Zechariah the fourth; all the sons and kindred of Hosah were thirteen.
text.Rotherham: Hilkiah the second, Tebaliah the third, Zechariah the fourth,—all the sons and brethren of Hosah, were thirteen.
text.KJV: 26:11 Hilkiah the second, Tebaliah the thirde, Zechariah the fourth: all the sonnes and brethren of Hosah, were thirteene.
text.NABRE: 26:11 Hilkiah, the second son, Tebaliah, the third, Zechariah, the fourth. All the sons and kinsmen of Hosah were thirteen.

text.NRSV: These divisions of the gatekeepers, through their chief men, had duties, just as their kindred did, ministering in the house of the Lord;
text.Rotherham: To these, belonged the courses of door-keepers, to the chiefs of the strong men, belonged charges, equally with their brethren,—to be in attendance in the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 26:12 Among these were the diuisions of the porters, euen among the chiefe men, hauing wards one against another, to minister in the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 26:12 To these divisions of the gatekeepers, by their chief men, were assigned watches for them to minister in the house of the Lord, for each group in the same way.

text.NRSV: and they cast lots by ancestral houses, small and great alike, for their gates.
text.Rotherham: Yea they cast lots, as the small so the great, by their ancestral house, for every several gate.
text.KJV: 26:13 ¶ And they cast lots as well the small as the great, according to the house of their fathers for euery gate.
text.NABRE: 26:13 They cast lots for each gate, small and large families alike.

text.NRSV: The lot for the east fell to Shelemiah. Then they cast lots for his son Zechariah, a prudent counselor, and his lot came out for the north.
text.Rotherham: And the lot eastward fell to Shelemiah. And, for Zechariah his son, a discreet counsellor, they cast lots, and his lot came out, northward.
text.KJV: 26:14 And the lot Eastward fel to Shelemiah; then for Zechariah his sonne (a wise counseller:) they cast lots, and his lot came out Northward.
text.NABRE: 26:14 When the lot was cast for the east side, it fell to Meshelemiah. Then they cast lots for his son Zechariah, a prudent counselor, and the north side fell to his lot.

text.NRSV: Obed-edom’s came out for the south, and to his sons was allotted the storehouse.
text.Rotherham: To Obed-edom, southward,—and, to his sons, the storehouse.
text.KJV: 26:15 To Obed-Edom Southward, and to his sonnes, the house of Asuppim.
text.NABRE: 26:15 To Obed-edom fell the south side, and to his sons the storehouse.

text.NRSV: For Shuppim and Hosah it came out for the west, at the gate of Shallecheth on the ascending road. Guard corresponded to guard.
text.Rotherham: To Shuppim and to Hosah, westward, near the refuse-gate, in the causeway that goeth up,—one ward as well as another.
text.KJV: 26:16 To Shuppim and Hosa, the lot came foorth Westward with the gate Shallecheth, by the causey of the going vp, ward against ward.
text.NABRE: 26:16 To Hosah fell the west side with the Shallecheth gate at the ascending highway. For each family, watches were established.

text.NRSV: On the east there were six Levites each day, on the north four each day, on the south four each day, as well as two and two at the storehouse;
text.Rotherham: Eastward—daily, were six,—northward—daily, four, southward—daily, four, and, for the stores, two and two.
text.KJV: 26:17 Eastward were sixe Leuites, Northward foure a day, Southward foure a day, and toward Asuppim two and two.
text.NABRE: 26:17 On the east, six watched each day, on the north, four each day, on the south, four each day, and at the storehouse they were two and two;

text.NRSV: and for the colonnade on the west there were four at the road and two at the colonnade.
text.Rotherham: For the precinct—westward, four, at the causeway, two—by the precinct.
text.KJV: 26:18 And Parbar Westward, foure at the causey, and two at Parbar.
text.NABRE: 26:18 as for the large building on the west, there were four at the highway and two at the large building.

text.NRSV: These were the divisions of the gatekeepers among the Korahites and the sons of Merari.
text.Rotherham: These, were the courses of the door-keepers, of the sons of the Korahites, and of the sons of Merari.
text.KJV: 26:19 These are the diuisions of the porters among the sonnes of Kore, and among the sonnes of Merari.
text.NABRE: 26:19 These were the classes of the gatekeepers, sons of Korah and Merari.

text.NRSV: And of the Levites, Ahijah had charge of the treasuries of the house of God and the treasuries of the dedicated gifts.
text.Rotherham: And, the Levites, their brethren, were over the treasuries of the house of God, even to the treasuries of hallowed things.
text.KJV: 26:20 ¶ And of the Leuites, Ahiiah was ouer the treasures of the house of God, and ouer the treasures of the dedicate things.
text.NABRE: 26:20 Treasurers. Their brother Levites had oversight of the treasuries of the house of God and the treasuries of votive offerings.

text.NRSV: The sons of Ladan, the sons of the Gershonites belonging to Ladan, the heads of ancestral houses belonging to Ladan the Gershonite: Jehieli.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Ladan—the sons of the Gershonites belonging to Ladan—the ancestral chiefs belonging to Ladan the Gershonite, were Jehieli;
text.KJV: 26:21 As concerning the sonnes of Laadan: the sonnes of the Gershonite Laadan, hiefe fathers; euen of Laadan the Gershonite, were Iehieli.
text.NABRE: 26:21 Among the sons of Ladan the Gershonite, the family heads were sons of Jehiel:

text.NRSV: The sons of Jehieli, Zetham and his brother Joel, had charge of the treasuries of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: the sons of Jehieli, Zetham and Joel his brother who were over the treasuries of the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 26:22 The sonnes of Iehieli, Zetham and Ioel his brother, which were ouer the treasures of the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 26:22 the sons of Jehiel, Zetham and his brother Joel, who oversaw the treasures of the house of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Of the Amramites, the Izharites, the Hebronites, and the Uzzielites:
text.Rotherham: Then came certain of the Amramites, of the Izharites, of the Hebronites, of the Uzzielites.
text.KJV: 26:23 Of the Amramites, and the Izharites, the Hebronites, and the Uzzielites:
text.NABRE: 26:23 Of the Amramites, Izharites, Hebronites, and Uzzielites,

text.NRSV: Shebuel son of Gershom, son of Moses, was chief officer in charge of the treasuries.
text.Rotherham: And, Shebuel, son of Gershom, son of Moses, was chief ruler over the treasuries.
text.KJV: 26:24 And Shebuel the sonne of Gershom, the sonne of Moses, was ruler of the treasures.
text.NABRE: 26:24 Shubael, son of Gershom, son of Moses, was principal overseer of the treasures.

text.NRSV: His kindred by Eliezer: his son Rehabiah, and his son Jeshaiah, and his son Joram, and his son Zichri, and his son Shelomoth.
text.Rotherham: And, his brethren pertaining to Eliezer, were Rehabiah his son, and Jeshaiah his son, and Joram his son, and Zichri his son, and Shelomoth his son.
text.KJV: 26:25 And his brethren by Eliezer: Rehabiah his sonne, and Ieshaiah his sonne, and Ioram his sonne, and Zichri his sonne, & Shelomith his sonne.
text.NABRE: 26:25 His associate was of the line of Eliezer, whose son was Rehabiah, whose son was Jeshaiah, whose son was Joram, whose son was Zichri, whose son was Shelomith.

text.NRSV: This Shelomoth and his kindred were in charge of all the treasuries of the dedicated gifts that King David, and the heads of ancestral houses, and the officers of the thousands and the hundreds, and the commanders of the army, had dedicated.
text.Rotherham: The same Shelomoth and his brethren, were over all the treasuries of the hallowed things, which David the king and the ancestral chiefs, with the captains of thousands and hundreds and the captains of the army, had hallowed:
text.KJV: 26:26 Which Shelomith and his brethren, were ouer all the treasures of the dedicate things, which Dauid the king and the chiefe fathers, the captaines ouer thousands and hundreds, and the captaines of the hoste had dedicated.
text.NABRE: 26:26 This Shelomith and his kinsmen oversaw all the treasures of the votive offerings dedicated by King David, the heads of the families, the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and the commanders of the army;

text.NRSV: From booty won in battles they dedicated gifts for the maintenance of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: out of the battles and out of the spoil, had they hallowed [them],—to strengthen [the provision] for the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 26:27 Out of the spoyles wonne in battels, did they dedicate to maintaine the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 26:27 what came from wars and from spoils, they dedicated for the support of the house of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Also all that Samuel the seer, and Saul son of Kish, and Abner son of Ner, and Joab son of Zeruiah had dedicated—all dedicated gifts were in the care of Shelomoth and his kindred.
text.Rotherham: And, all that Samuel the seer, and Saul son of Kish, and Abner son of Ner, and Joab son of Zeruiah, had hallowed, whosoever had hallowed anything, it was under the direction of Shelomoth, and his brethren.
text.KJV: 26:28 And all that Samuel the Seer, and Saul the sonne of Kish, and Abner the sonne of Ner, and Ioab the sonne of Zeruiah had dedicated, and whosoeuer had dedicated any thing, it was vnder the hand of Shelomith and of his brethren.
text.NABRE: 26:28 Also, whatever Samuel the seer, Saul, son of Kish, Abner, son of Ner, Joab, son of Zeruiah, and all others had consecrated, was under the charge of Shelomith and his kinsmen.

text.NRSV: Of the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons were appointed to outside duties for Israel, as officers and judges.
text.Rotherham: Of the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons, were for the outward business over Israel,—as officers, and as judges.
text.KJV: 26:29 ¶ Of the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sonnes, were for the outward busines ouer Israel, for officers and Iudges.
text.NABRE: 26:29 Magistrates. Among the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons were in charge of Israel’s civil affairs as officials and judges.

text.NRSV: Of the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his kindred, one thousand seven hundred able men, had the oversight of Israel west of the Jordan for all the work of the Lord and for the service of the king.
text.Rotherham: Of the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his brethren, sons of valour, one thousand seven hundred, had the oversight of Israel, across the Jordan westward,—for all the business of Yahweh, and for the service of the king.
text.KJV: 26:30 And of the Hebronits, Hashabiah and his brethren, men of valour, a thousand and seuen hundred, were officers among them of Israel on this side Iorden westward, in all busines of the Lord, and in the seruice of the king.
text.NABRE: 26:30 Among the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his kinsmen, one thousand seven hundred men of substance, had the administration of Israel on the western side of the Jordan for all the work of the Lord and the service of the king.

text.NRSV: Of the Hebronites, Jerijah was chief of the Hebronites of whatever genealogy or ancestral house. In the fortieth year of David’s reign search was made and men of great ability among them were found at Jazer in Gilead.
text.Rotherham: Of the Hebronites, Jerijah, was chief, [he was] of the Hebronites by his generations, by his fathers,—in the fortieth year of the reign of David, were they sought out, and there were found among them heroes of valour, in Jazer of Gilead;
text.KJV: 26:31 Among the Hebronites was Ieriiah the chiefe, euen among the Hebronits, according to the generations of his fathers: in the fourtieth yeere of the reigne of Dauid, they were sought for, and there were found among them mightie men of valour, at Iazer of Gilead.
text.NABRE: 26:31 Among the Hebronites, Jerijah was their chief according to their family records. In the fortieth year of David’s reign search was made, and there were found among them warriors at Jazer of Gilead.

text.NRSV: King David appointed him and his kindred, two thousand seven hundred able men, heads of ancestral houses, to have the oversight of the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of the Manassites, for everything pertaining to God and for the affairs of the king.
text.Rotherham: and, his brethren that were heroes of valour, were two thousand and seven hundred, ancestral chiefs,—so David the king gave them oversight, over the Reubenites and the Gadites and the half tribe of Manassites, in all the affairs of God, and the affairs of the king.
text.KJV: 26:32 And his brethren, men of valour, were two thousand and seuen hundred chiefe fathers, whom King Dauid made rulers ouer the Reubenites, the Gadites, & the halfe tribe of Manasseh, for euery matter perteining to God, and affaires of the king.
text.NABRE: 26:32 His kinsmen were also men of substance, two thousand seven hundred heads of families. King David appointed them to the administration of the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh for everything pertaining to God and to the king.

text.NRSV: This is the list of the people of Israel, the heads of ancestral houses, the commanders of the thousands and the hundreds, and their officers who served the king in all matters of the divisions that came on duty and went off duty month by month throughout the year, each division numbering twenty-four thousand:
text.Rotherham: Now, the sons of Israel—as to the number of them, the ancestral chiefs—and rulers of thousands and hundreds, and their officers who waited upon the king as to any matter of the courses, who came in and went out month by month, for all the months of the year, in each course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: 27:1 Nowe the children of Israel after their number, to wit, the chiefe fathers and captaines of thousands and hundreds, and their officers that serued the king in any matter of the courses, which came in, and went out moneth by moneth, throughout all the moneths of the yeare, of euery course were twentie and foure thousand.
text.NABRE: 27:1 Chapter 27 Army Commanders. This is the list of the Israelite family heads, commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and other officers who served the king in all that pertained to the divisions, of twenty-four thousand men each, that came and went month by month throughout the year.

text.NRSV: Jashobeam son of Zabdiel was in charge of the first division, for the first month; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: Over the first course, for the first month, was Jashobeam, son of Zabdiel,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: 27:2 Ouer the first course for the first moneth was Iashobeam the sonne of Zabdiel, and in his course were twentie and foure thousand.
text.NABRE: 27:2 Over the first division for the first month was Ishbaal, son of Zabdiel, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men;

text.NRSV: He was a descendant of Perez, and was chief of all the commanders of the army for the first month.
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Perez, was the chief for all the rulers of the hosts, for the first month.
text.KJV: 27:3 Of the children of Perez, was the chiefe of all the captaines of the host, for the first moneth.
text.NABRE: 27:3 a descendant of Perez, he was chief over all the commanders of the army for the first month.

text.NRSV: Dodai the Ahohite was in charge of the division of the second month; Mikloth was the chief officer of his division. In his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: And, over the course for the second month, was Dodai an Ahohite, and, of his course, was Mikloth also a chief ruler,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: 27:4 And ouer the course of the second moneth was Dodai an Ahohite, and of his course was Mikloth also the ruler: In his course likewise were twentie and foure thousand.
text.NABRE: 27:4 Over the division of the second month was Eleazar, son of Dodo, from Ahoh, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.

text.NRSV: The third commander, for the third month, was Benaiah son of the chief priest Jehoiada; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The ruler of the third host, for the third month, was Benaiah, son of Jehoiada the priest—a chief,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: 27:5 The third captaine of the host for the third month was Benaiah the sonne of Iehoiada a chiefe priest, and in his course were twenty and foure thousand.
text.NABRE: 27:5 The third army commander, for the third month, was Benaiah, son of Jehoiada the chief priest, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.

text.NRSV: This is the Benaiah who was a mighty warrior among the Thirty and in command of the Thirty; his son Ammizabad was in charge of his division.
text.Rotherham: The same Benaiah, was a hero of thirty, and over the thirty,—and, over his course, was Ammizabad his son.
text.KJV: 27:6 This is that Benaiah, who was mightie among the thirtie, and aboue the thirty: and in his course was Amizabad his sonne.
text.NABRE: 27:6 This Benaiah was a warrior among the Thirty and over the Thirty. His son Ammizabad was over his division.

text.NRSV: Asahel brother of Joab was fourth, for the fourth month, and his son Zebadiah after him; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The fourth, for the fourth month, was Asahel, brother of Joab, and Zebadiah his son, after him,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: 27:7 The fourth captaine for the fourth moneth was Asahel the brother of Ioab, and Zebadiah his sonne after him: and in his course were twentie and foure thousand.
text.NABRE: 27:7 Fourth, for the fourth month, was Asahel, brother of Joab, and after him his son Zebadiah, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.

text.NRSV: The fifth commander, for the fifth month, was Shamhuth the Izrahite; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: Of the fifth, for the fifth month, the ruler, was Shamhuth the Izrahite,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: 27:8 The fifth captaine for the fifth moneth, was Shamhuth the Izrahite: and in his course were twentie and foure thousand.
text.NABRE: 27:8 Fifth, for the fifth month, was the commander Shamhuth, a descendant of Zerah, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.

text.NRSV: Sixth, for the sixth month, was Ira son of Ikkesh of Tekoa; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The sixth, for the sixth month, was Ira son of Ikkesh, the Tekoite,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: 27:9 The sixt captaine for the sixt moneth, was Ira the son of Ikkesh the Tekoite: and in his course were twentie and foure thousand.
text.NABRE: 27:9 Sixth, for the sixth month, was Ira, son of Ikkesh, from Tekoa, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.

text.NRSV: Seventh, for the seventh month, was Helez the Pelonite, of the Ephraimites; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The seventh, for the seventh month, was Helez the Pelonite, of the sons of Ephraim,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: 27:10 The seuenth captaine for the seuenth moneth was Helez the Pelonite, of the children of Ephraim: and in his course were twentie and foure thousand.
text.NABRE: 27:10 Seventh, for the seventh month, was Hellez, from Beth-pelet, of the Ephraimites, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.

text.NRSV: Eighth, for the eighth month, was Sibbecai the Hushathite, of the Zerahites; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The eighth, for the eighth month, was Sibbecai the Hushathite, of the Zerahites,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: 27:11 The eighth captaine for the eighth moneth, was Sibbecai the Hushathite, of the Zarhites: and in his course were twentie and foure thousand.
text.NABRE: 27:11 Eighth, for the eighth month, was Sibbecai the Hushathite, a descendant of Zerah, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.

text.NRSV: Ninth, for the ninth month, was Abiezer of Anathoth, a Benjaminite; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The ninth, for the ninth month, was Abiezer the Anathothite, of the Benjamites,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: 27:12 The ninth captaine for the ninth moneth, was Abiezer the Anetothite, of the Beniamites: and in his course were twentie and foure thousand.
text.NABRE: 27:12 Ninth, for the ninth month, was Abiezer from Anathoth, of Benjamin, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.

text.NRSV: Tenth, for the tenth month, was Maharai of Netophah, of the Zerahites; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The tenth, for the tenth month, was Maharai the Netophathite, of the Zerahites,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: 27:13 The tenth captaine for the tenth moneth, was Maharai the Netophathite, of the Zarhites: and in his course were twentie and foure thousand.
text.NABRE: 27:13 Tenth, for the tenth month, was Maharai from Netophah, a descendant of Zerah, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.

text.NRSV: Eleventh, for the eleventh month, was Benaiah of Pirathon, of the Ephraimites; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The eleventh, for the eleventh month, was Benaiah the Pirathonite, of the sons of Ephraim,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: 27:14 The eleuenth captaine for the eleuenth moneth was Benaiah the Pirathonite, of the children of Ephraim: and in his course were twenty and foure thousand.
text.NABRE: 27:14 Eleventh, for the eleventh month, was Benaiah the Pirathonite, of the Ephraimites, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.

text.NRSV: Twelfth, for the twelfth month, was Heldai the Netophathite, of Othniel; in his division were twenty-four thousand.
text.Rotherham: The twelfth, for the twelfth month, was Heldai the Netophathite, of Othniel,—and, in his course, were twenty-four thousand.
text.KJV: 27:15 The twelfth captaine for the twelfth moneth, was Heldai the Netophathite, of Othniel: and in his course were twentie and foure thousand.
text.NABRE: 27:15 Twelfth, for the twelfth month, was Heldai the Netophathite, of the family of Othniel, and in his division were twenty-four thousand men.

text.NRSV: Over the tribes of Israel, for the Reubenites, Eliezer son of Zichri was chief officer; for the Simeonites, Shephatiah son of Maacah;
text.Rotherham: Furthermore, over the tribes of Israel, the chief ruler of the Reubenites, was Eliezer, son of Zichri. Of the Simeonites, Shephatiah, son of Maacah.
text.KJV: 27:16 ¶ Furthermore, ouer the tribes of Israel: The Ruler of the Reubenites was Eliezer the sonne of Zichri: of the Simeonites, Shephatiah the sonne of Maachah.
text.NABRE: 27:16 Tribal Leaders. Over the tribes of Israel, for the Reubenites the leader was Eliezer, son of Zichri; for the Simeonites, Shephatiah, son of Maacah;

text.NRSV: for Levi, Hashabiah son of Kemuel; for Aaron, Zadok;
text.Rotherham: Of Levi, Hashabiah, son of Kemuel. Of Aaron, Zadok.
text.KJV: 27:17 Of the Leuites: Hashabiah the sonne of Kemuel; of the Aaronites, Zadok.
text.NABRE: 27:17 for Levi, Hashabiah, son of Kemuel; for Aaron, Zadok;

text.NRSV: for Judah, Elihu, one of David’s brothers; for Issachar, Omri son of Michael;
text.Rotherham: Of Judah, Elihu, one of the brethren of David. Of Issachar, Omri, son of Michael.
text.KJV: 27:18 Of Iudah, Elihu, one of the brethren of Dauid: of Issachar, Omri the sonne of Michael.
text.NABRE: 27:18 for Judah, Eliab, one of David’s brothers; for Issachar, Omri, son of Michael;

text.NRSV: for Zebulun, Ishmaiah son of Obadiah; for Naphtali, Jerimoth son of Azriel;
text.Rotherham: Of Zebulun, Ishmaiah, son of Obadiah. Of Naphtali, Jeremoth, son of Azriel.
text.KJV: 27:19 Of Zebulun, Ishmaiah the son of Obadiah: of Naphtali, Ierimoth the sonne of Azriel.
text.NABRE: 27:19 for Zebulun, Ishmaiah, son of Obadiah; for Naphtali, Jeremoth, son of Azriel;

text.NRSV: for the Ephraimites, Hoshea son of Azaziah; for the half-tribe of Manasseh, Joel son of Pedaiah;
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Ephraim, Hoshea son of Azaziah. Of the half tribe of Manasseh, Joel, son of Pedaiah.
text.KJV: 27:20 Of the children of Ephraim, Hoshea the sonne of Azazziah: of the halfe tribe of Manasseh, Ioel the sonne of Pedaiah.
text.NABRE: 27:20 for the Ephraimites, Hoshea, son of Azaziah; for the half-tribe of Manasseh, Joel, son of Pedaiah;

text.NRSV: for the half-tribe of Manasseh in Gilead, Iddo son of Zechariah; for Benjamin, Jaasiel son of Abner;
text.Rotherham: Of the half of Manasseh, in Gilead, Iddo, son of Zechariah. Of Benjamin, Jaasiel, son of Abner.
text.KJV: 27:21 Of the halfe tribe of Manasseh in Gilead, Iddo the sonne of Zechariah: of Beniamin, Iaasiel the son of Abner.
text.NABRE: 27:21 for the half-tribe of Manasseh in Gilead, Iddo, son of Zechariah; for Benjamin, Jaasiel, son of Abner;

text.NRSV: for Dan, Azarel son of Jeroham. These were the leaders of the tribes of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Of Dan, Azarel, son of Jeroham. These, were the rulers of the tribes of Israel.
text.KJV: 27:22 Of Dan, Azariel the sonne of Ieroham. These were the princes of the tribes of Israel.
text.NABRE: 27:22 for Dan, Azarel, son of Jeroham. These were the commanders of the tribes of Israel.

text.NRSV: David did not count those below twenty years of age, for the Lord had promised to make Israel as numerous as the stars of heaven.
text.Rotherham: But David took not the number of them, from twenty years old, and under,—because Yahweh had said, he would multiply Israel like the stars of the heavens.
text.KJV: 27:23 ¶ But Dauid tooke not the number of them from twentie yeeres olde and vnder: because the Lord had said, hee would increase Israel like to the starres of the heauens.
text.NABRE: 27:23 David did not count those who were twenty years of age or younger, for the Lord had promised to multiply Israel like the stars of the heavens.

text.NRSV: Joab son of Zeruiah began to count them, but did not finish; yet wrath came upon Israel for this, and the number was not entered into the account of the Annals of King David.
text.Rotherham: Joab son of Zeruiah, began to number, but finished not, when there arose, on this account, indignation against Israel,—neither did the number come up into the account of the chronicles of King David.
text.KJV: 27:24 Ioab the sonne of Zeruiah began to number, but he finished not, because there fell wrath for it against Israel, neither was the number put in the account of the Chronicles of King Dauid.
text.NABRE: 27:24 Joab, son of Zeruiah, began to take the census, but he did not complete it, for because of it wrath fell upon Israel. Therefore the number was not recorded in the book of chronicles of King David.

text.NRSV: Over the king’s treasuries was Azmaveth son of Adiel. Over the treasuries in the country, in the cities, in the villages, and in the towers, was Jonathan son of Uzziah.
text.Rotherham: And, over the treasuries of the king, was Azmaveth, son of Adiel. And, over the treasuries in the fields, in the cities, in the villages and in the castles, was Jonathan, son of Uzziah;
text.KJV: 27:25 ¶ And ouer the Kings treasures, was Azmaueth the sonne of Adiel: and ouer the store-houses in the fields, in the cities, and in the villages, and in the castles, was Iehonathan the sonne of Uzziah.
text.NABRE: 27:25 Overseers. Over the treasuries of the king was Azmaveth, the son of Adiel. Over the treasuries in the country, the cities, the villages, and the towers was Jonathan, son of Uzziah.

text.NRSV: Over those who did the work of the field, tilling the soil, was Ezri son of Chelub.
text.Rotherham: And, over the workers of the field, for the tillage of the ground, was Ezri, son of Chelub.
text.KJV: 27:26 And ouer them that did the worke of the field, for tillage of the ground, was Ezri the sonne of Chelub.
text.NABRE: 27:26 Over the farm workers who tilled the soil was Ezri, son of Chelub.

text.NRSV: Over the vineyards was Shimei the Ramathite. Over the produce of the vineyards for the wine cellars was Zabdi the Shiphmite.
text.Rotherham: And, over the vineyards, was Shimei, the Ramathite. And, over that which was in the vineyards, for the treasuries of wine, was Zabdi, the Shiphmite.
text.KJV: 27:27 And ouer the Uineyards, was Shimei the Ramathite: ouer the increase of the vineyards for the wine cellars, was Sabdi the Ziphmite.
text.NABRE: 27:27 Over the vineyards was Shimei from Ramah, and over their produce for the wine cellars was Zabdi the Shiphmite.

text.NRSV: Over the olive and sycamore trees in the Shephelah was Baal-hanan the Gederite. Over the stores of oil was Joash.
text.Rotherham: And, over the olive-trees and the sycamore-trees that were in the lowland, was Baal-hanan the Gederite. And, over the treasuries of oil, was Joash.
text.KJV: 27:28 And ouer the Oliue trees, and the Sycomore trees that were in the lowe plaines, was Baal-hanan the Gederite: and ouer the cellars of oyle was Ioash.
text.NABRE: 27:28 Over the olive trees and sycamores of the Shephelah was Baalhanan the Gederite, and over the stores of oil was Joash.

text.NRSV: Over the herds that pastured in Sharon was Shitrai the Sharonite. Over the herds in the valleys was Shaphat son of Adlai.
text.Rotherham: And, over the herds that pastured in Sharon, was Shitrai, the Sharonite. And, over the herds in the vales, Shaphat, son of Adlai.
text.KJV: 27:29 And ouer the herdes that fed in Sharon, was Shetrai the Sharonite: and ouer the herds that were in the valleys, was Shaphat the sonne of Adlai.
text.NABRE: 27:29 Over the cattle that grazed in Sharon was Shitrai the Sharonite, and over the cattle in the valleys was Shaphat, the son of Adlai;

text.NRSV: Over the camels was Obil the Ishmaelite. Over the donkeys was Jehdeiah the Meronothite.
text.Rotherham: And, over the camels, was Obil, the Ishmaelite. And, over the asses, was Jehdeyahu the Meronothite.
text.KJV: 27:30 Ouer the camels also, was Obil the Ishmaelite: and ouer the Asses, was Iehdeiah the Meronothite.
text.NABRE: 27:30 over the camels was Obil the Ishmaelite; over the donkeys was Jehdeiah the Meronothite;

text.NRSV: Over the flocks was Jaziz the Hagrite. All these were stewards of King David’s property.
text.Rotherham: And, over the flocks, was Jaziz, the Hagrite. All these, were rulers over the possessions that belonged to King David.
text.KJV: 27:31 And ouer the flockes, was Iaziz the Hagerite. All these were the rulers of the substance which was king Dauids.
text.NABRE: 27:31 and over the flocks was Jaziz the Hagrite. All these were the overseers of King David’s possessions.

text.NRSV: Jonathan, David’s uncle, was a counselor, being a man of understanding and a scribe; Jehiel son of Hachmoni attended the king’s sons.
text.Rotherham: And, Jonathan the relative of David, was a counsellor, a man of understanding and a scribe, was he. And, Jehiel son of Hachmoni, was with the sons of the king.
text.KJV: 27:32 Also Ionathan Dauids uncle, was a counseller, a wise man, and a Scribe: and Iehiel the sonne of Hachmoni, was with the kings sonnes.
text.NABRE: 27:32 David’s Court. Jonathan, David’s uncle and a man of intelligence, was counselor and scribe; he and Jehiel, the son of Hachmoni, attended the king’s sons.

text.NRSV: Ahithophel was the king’s counselor, and Hushai the Archite was the king’s friend.
text.Rotherham: And, Ahitophel, was counsellor to the king. And, Hushai the Archite, was the companion of the king.
text.KJV: 27:33 And Ahitophel was the kings counseller, and Hushai the Archite, was the kings companion.
text.NABRE: 27:33 Ahithophel was also the king’s counselor, and Hushai the Archite was the king’s friend.

text.NRSV: Ahithophel was succeeded by Jehoiada son of Benaiah, and Abiathar. Joab was commander of the king’s army.
text.Rotherham: And, after Ahitophel, was Jehoiada son of Benaiah—and Abiathar. And, the captain of the king’s army, was Joab.
text.KJV: 27:34 And after Ahitophel, was Iehoiada the sonne of Benaiah, and Abiathar: and the general of the Kings armie was Ioab.
text.NABRE: 27:34 After Ahithophel came Jehoiada, the son of Benaiah, and Abiathar. The commander of the king’s army was Joab.

text.NRSV: David assembled at Jerusalem all the officials of Israel, the officials of the tribes, the officers of the divisions that served the king, the commanders of the thousands, the commanders of the hundreds, the stewards of all the property and cattle of the king and his sons, together with the palace officials, the mighty warriors, and all the warriors.
text.Rotherham: And David called together all the rulers of Israel, the rulers of tribes and the rulers of the courses who waited upon the king,—and the rulers of thousands and the rulers of hundreds, and the rulers over all the possessions and substance that belonged to the king and to his sons, with the courtiers and the heroes, even every hero of valour, unto Jerusalem.
text.KJV: 28:1 And Dauid assembled all the Princes of Israel, the Princes of the tribes, and the captains of the companies that ministred to the king by course: and the captaines ouer the thousands, and captaines ouer the hundreds, and the stewards ouer all the substance and possession of the King, and of his sonnes, with the officers, and with the mightie men, and with all the valiant men, vnto Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: 28:1 Chapter 28 The Assembly at Jerusalem. David assembled at Jerusalem all the commanders of Israel, the tribal commanders, the commanders of the divisions who were in the service of the king, the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, those in command of all the king’s estates and possessions, and his sons, together with the courtiers, the warriors, and every person of substance.

text.NRSV: Then King David rose to his feet and said: “Hear me, my brothers and my people. I had it in my heart to build a house of rest for the ark of the covenant of the Lord, for the footstool of our God; and I made preparations for building.
text.Rotherham: Then David the king rose up on his feet, and said, Hear me, my brethren and my people,—As for me, it was near my heart, to build a house of rest for the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, and for the footstool of our God, and I made ready to build.
text.KJV: 28:2 Then Dauid the king stood vp vpon his feete, and said, Heare me, my brethren, and my people: As for me, I had in mine heart to builde an house of rest for the Arke of the Couenant of the Lord, and for the footestoole of our God, & had made ready for the building.
text.NABRE: 28:2 King David rose to his feet and said: “Hear me, my kinsmen and my people. It was my purpose to build a house of repose myself for the ark of the covenant of the Lord, the footstool for the feet of our God; and I was preparing to build it.

text.NRSV: But God said to me, ‘You shall not build a house for my name, for you are a warrior and have shed blood.’
text.Rotherham: But, God, said to me, Thou shalt not build a house for my Name,—for, a man of war, thou art, and, blood, hast thou shed.
text.KJV: 28:3 But God said vnto me, Thou shalt not builde an house for my Name, because thou hast been a man of warre, and hast shed blood.
text.NABRE: 28:3 But God said to me, You may not build a house for my name, for you are a man who waged wars and shed blood.

text.NRSV: Yet the Lord God of Israel chose me from all my ancestral house to be king over Israel forever; for he chose Judah as leader, and in the house of Judah my father’s house, and among my father’s sons he took delight in making me king over all Israel.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit Yahweh, God of Israel, made choice of me, out of all of the house of my father, to become king over Israel unto times age-abiding, for, of Judah, had he made choice to be chief ruler, and, in the house of Judah, the house of my father,—and, among the sons of my father, with me, was he well-pleased, to make me king over all Israel;
text.KJV: 28:4 Howbeit, the Lord God of Israel chose me before all the house of my father, to be king ouer Israel for euer: for he hath chosen Iudah to be the ruler; & of the house of Iudah, the house of my father; and among the sonnes of my father, he liked me to make me king ouer all Israel:
text.NABRE: 28:4 However, the Lord, the God of Israel, chose me from all my father’s family to be king over Israel forever. For he chose Judah as leader, then one family of Judah, that of my father; and finally, among all the sons of my father, it pleased him to make me king over all Israel.

text.NRSV: And of all my sons (for the Lord has given me many sons) he has chosen my son Solomon to sit upon the throne of the kingdom of the Lord over Israel.
text.Rotherham: and, from among all my sons,—for, many sons, hath Yahweh given me,—he hath made choice of Solomon my son, to sit upon the throne of the kingdom of Yahweh, over Israel.
text.KJV: 28:5 And of all my sonnes (for the Lord hath giuen me many sonnes) hee hath chosen Solomon my sonne, to sit vpon the throne of the kingdome of the Lord ouer Israel.
text.NABRE: 28:5 And of all my sons—for the Lord has given me many sons—he has chosen my son Solomon to sit on the throne of the Lord ’s kingship over Israel.

text.NRSV: He said to me, ‘It is your son Solomon who shall build my house and my courts, for I have chosen him to be a son to me, and I will be a father to him.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto am, Solomon thy son—he, shall build my house, and my courts,—for I have made choice of him that he may be my son, and that, I, may be his father.
text.KJV: 28:6 And he said vnto me, Solomon thy sonne, hee shall build my house and my courts: for I haue chosen him to be my sonne, and I will be his father.
text.NABRE: 28:6 For he said to me: It is your son Solomon who shall build my house and my courts, for I have chosen him for my son, and I will be a father to him.

text.NRSV: I will establish his kingdom forever if he continues resolute in keeping my commandments and my ordinances, as he is today.’
text.Rotherham: So will I establish his kingdom, unto times age-abiding,—if he be strong, to do my commandments and my regulations, as at this day.
text.KJV: 28:7 Moreouer, I will establish his kingdome for euer, if he be constant to do my commandements and my iudgements, as at this day.
text.NABRE: 28:7 I will establish his kingdom forever, if he perseveres in carrying out my commandments and ordinances as he does now.

text.NRSV: Now therefore in the sight of all Israel, the assembly of the Lord, and in the hearing of our God, observe and search out all the commandments of the Lord your God; that you may possess this good land, and leave it for an inheritance to your children after you forever.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, in the eyes of all Israel—the convocation of Yahweh, and in the ears of our God, Observe and seek all the commandments of Yahweh your God,—to the end that ye may possess the good land,—and suffer your sons after you to inherit it, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: 28:8 Now therefore in the sight of all Israel, the congregation of the Lord, and in the audience of our God, keepe, and seeke for all the commandements of the Lord your God, that ye may possesse this good land, and leaue it for an inheritance for your children after you, for euer.
text.NABRE: 28:8 Therefore, in the sight of all Israel, the assembly of the Lord, and in the hearing of our God: keep and carry out all the commandments of the Lord, your God, that you may continue to possess this good land and afterward leave it as an inheritance to your children forever.

text.NRSV: “And you, my son Solomon, know the God of your father, and serve him with a single mind and a willing heart; for the Lord searches every mind, and understands every plan and thought. If you seek him, he will be found by you; but if you forsake him, he will abandon you forever.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, Solomon my son, Know thou the God of thy father, and serve him with a perfect heart and with a willing soul, for, all hearts, doth Yahweh search, and, every devised purpose, doth he understand,—If thou seek him, He will be found of thee, but, if thou forsake him, He will cast thee off for ever.
text.KJV: 28:9 ¶ And thou, Solomon my sonne, know thou the God of thy father, and serue him with a perfite heart, and with a willing minde: for the Lord searcheth all hearts, and vnderstandeth all the imaginations of the thoughts: if thou seeke him, he will be found of thee, but if thou forsake him, he will cast thee off for euer.
text.NABRE: 28:9 “As for you, Solomon, my son, know the God of your father and serve him with a whole heart and a willing soul, for the Lord searches all hearts and understands all the mind’s thoughts. If you search for him, he will be found; but if you abandon him, he will cast you off forever.

text.NRSV: Take heed now, for the Lord has chosen you to build a house as the sanctuary; be strong, and act.”
text.Rotherham: See! now, that, Yahweh, hath made choice of thee, to build a house for a sanctuary—be strong and do!
text.KJV: 28:10 Take heed now, for the Lord hath chosen thee to builde an house for the Sanctuarie: be strong, and doe it.
text.NABRE: 28:10 See, then! The Lord has chosen you to build a house as his sanctuary. Be strong and set to work.”

text.NRSV: Then David gave his son Solomon the plan of the vestibule of the temple, and of its houses, its treasuries, its upper rooms, and its inner chambers, and of the room for the mercy seat;
text.Rotherham: Then gave David unto Solomon his son, the plan of the porch, and the recesses thereof, and the treasuries thereof, and the upper rooms thereof, and the inner chambers thereof, and the recess for the propitiatory;
text.KJV: 28:11 ¶ Then Dauid gaue to Solomon his sonne the paterne of the porch, and of the houses thereof, and of the treasuries thereof, and of the vpper chambers thereof, and of the inner parlours thereof, and of the place of the Mercie-seate.
text.NABRE: 28:11 Temple Plans Given to Solomon. Then David gave to his son Solomon the design of the portico and of the house itself, with its storerooms, its upper rooms and inner chambers, and the shrine containing the cover of the ark.

text.NRSV: and the plan of all that he had in mind: for the courts of the house of the Lord, all the surrounding chambers, the treasuries of the house of God, and the treasuries for dedicated gifts;
text.Rotherham: and the plan of all which had come by the spirit to be with him, for the courts of the house of Yahweh, and for all the rooms round about,—for the treasuries of the house of God, and for the treasuries of hallowed things;
text.KJV: 28:12 And the paterne of all that hee had by the spirit, of the courts of the house of the Lord, and of all the chambers round about, of the treasuries of the house of God, and of the treasuries of the dedicate things:
text.NABRE: 28:12 He provided also the design for all else that he had in mind by way of courts for the house of the Lord, with the surrounding compartments for the treasuries of the house of God and the treasuries for the votive offerings,

text.NRSV: for the divisions of the priests and of the Levites, and all the work of the service in the house of the Lord; for all the vessels for the service in the house of the Lord,
text.Rotherham: and for the courses of the priests and the Levites, and for all the work of the service of the house of Yahweh,—and for all the utensils of the service of the house of Yahweh:
text.KJV: 28:13 Also for the courses of the Priests and the Leuites, & for all the worke of the seruice of the house of the Lord, and for all the vessels of seruice in the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 28:13 as well as for the divisions of the priests and Levites, for all the work of the service of the house of the Lord, and for all the liturgical vessels of the house of the Lord.

text.NRSV: the weight of gold for all golden vessels for each service, the weight of silver vessels for each service,
text.Rotherham: of gold, by weight, for the gold, for all manner of utensils for each several service,—for all manner of utensils of silver, by weight, for all manner of utensils for each several service;
text.KJV: 28:14 Hee gaue of golde by weight, for things of golde, for all instruments of all manner of seruice: siluer also for all instruments of siluer, by weight, for all instruments of euery kinde of seruice:
text.NABRE: 28:14 He specified the weight of gold to be used in the golden vessels for the various services and the weight of silver to be used in the silver vessels for the various services;

text.NRSV: the weight of the golden lampstands and their lamps, the weight of gold for each lampstand and its lamps, the weight of silver for a lampstand and its lamps, according to the use of each lampstand in the service,
text.Rotherham: and a weight, for the lampstands of gold, and their lamps of gold, by the weight of each several lampstand, and the lamps thereof,—and for the lampstands of silver by weight, for each lampstand and the lamps thereof, according to the service of each several lampstand.
text.KJV: 28:15 Euen the weight for the Candlestickes of golde, and for their lampes of golde, by weight for euery candlesticke, and for the lampes thereof: and for the Candlestickes of siluer by weight, both for the Candlesticke and also for the lampes thereof, according to the vse of euery candlesticke.
text.NABRE: 28:15 likewise for the golden menorahs and their lamps he specified the weight of gold for each menorah and its lamps, and for the silver menorahs he specified the weight of silver for each menorah and its lamps, depending on the use to which each menorah was to be put.

text.NRSV: the weight of gold for each table for the rows of bread, the silver for the silver tables,
text.Rotherham: And the gold [gave he] by weight for the tables for setting in array, for each several table,—and silver, for the tables of silver;
text.KJV: 28:16 And by weight hee gaue golde for the tables of shew-bread, for euery table, and likewise siluer for the tables of siluer.
text.NABRE: 28:16 He specified the weight of gold for each table that was to hold the showbread, and the silver for the silver tables;

text.NRSV: and pure gold for the forks, the basins, and the cups; for the golden bowls and the weight of each; for the silver bowls and the weight of each;
text.Rotherham: and the fleshhooks and the bowls and the cups, pure gold,—and for the goblets of gold, by weight for each several goblet, and for the goblets of silver, by weight for each several goblet;
text.KJV: 28:17 Also pure golde for the fleshhookes, and the bowles, and the cups: and for the golden basins hee gaue golde by weight, for euery basin; and likewise siluer by weight, for euery basin of siluer.
text.NABRE: 28:17 the pure gold for the forks, basins, and pitchers; the weight of gold for each golden bowl and the weight of silver for each silver bowl;

text.NRSV: for the altar of incense made of refined gold, and its weight; also his plan for the golden chariot of the cherubim that spread their wings and covered the ark of the covenant of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and, for the altar of incense, refined gold, by weight,—and, for the pattern of the chariot of the cherubim, gold, for them that were spreading out, and covering the ark of the covenant of Yahweh:—
text.KJV: 28:18 And for the Altar of incense, refined golde by weight; and gold for the paterne of the charet of the Cherubims, that spread out their wings, and couered the Arke of the Couenant of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 28:18 the refined gold, and its weight, to be used for the altar of incense; and, finally, gold to fashion the chariot: the cherubim spreading their wings and covering the ark of the covenant of the Lord.

text.NRSV: “All this, in writing at the Lord’s direction, he made clear to me—the plan of all the works.”
text.Rotherham: the whole in writing, From the hand of Yahweh upon me, to give understanding,—all the works of the pattern.
text.KJV: 28:19 All this, sayd Dauid, the Lord made mee understand in writing by his hand vpon mee, euen all the workes of this paterne.
text.NABRE: 28:19 All this he wrote down, by the hand of the Lord, to make him understand it—the working out of the whole design.

text.NRSV: David said further to his son Solomon, “Be strong and of good courage, and act. Do not be afraid or dismayed; for the Lord God, my God, is with you. He will not fail you or forsake you, until all the work for the service of the house of the Lord is finished.
text.Rotherham: Then said David, unto Solomon his son, Be strong and bold, and act, thou mayest not fear nor be dismayed,—for, Yahweh, God, my God, will be with thee, he will not let thee go, neither will he forsake thee, until thou hast finished all the work of the service of the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 28:20 And Dauid said to Solomon his sonne, Be strong, and of good courage, and doe it: feare not, nor be dismayed, for the Lord God, euen my God, will be with thee; he will not faile thee, nor forsake thee, vntill thou hast finished all the worke for the seruice of the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 28:20 Then David said to his son Solomon: “Be strong and steadfast, and go to work; do not fear or be dismayed, for the Lord God, my God, is with you. He will not fail you or abandon you before you have completed all the work for the service of the house of the Lord.

text.NRSV: The divisions of the priests and the Levites are ready for all the service of the house of God; and with you in all the work will be every volunteer who has skill for any kind of service; also the officers and all the people will be wholly at your command.”
text.Rotherham: And there are the courses of the priests and the Levites, for all the service of the house of God,—and, with thee, in all manner of work, is every willing, skilful man for any service, also the rulers and all the people, for all thine affairs.
text.KJV: 28:21 And behold, the courses of the Priests and the Leuites, euen they shall be with thee for all the seruice of the house of God, and there shall be with thee for all manner of workemanship, euery willing skilfull man, for any maner of seruice: also the Princes and all the people will bee wholly at thy commandement.
text.NABRE: 28:21 The divisions of the priests and Levites are ready for all the service of the house of God; they will be with you in all the work with all those who are eager to show their skill in every kind of craftsmanship. Also the commanders and all the people will do everything that you command.”

text.NRSV: King David said to the whole assembly, “My son Solomon, whom alone God has chosen, is young and inexperienced, and the work is great; for the temple will not be for mortals but for the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: Then said David the king unto all the convocation, Solomon my son, the one, of whom God hath made choice, is young and tender,—and, the work, is great, seeing that, not for man, is the palace, but for Yahweh Elohim.
text.KJV: 29:1 Furthermore, Dauid the King said vnto all the congregation, Solomon my sonne, whome alone God hath chosen, is yet young and tender, and the worke is great: for the palace is not for man, but for the Lord God.
text.NABRE: 29:1 Chapter 29 Offerings for the Temple. King David then said to the whole assembly: “My son Solomon, whom alone God has chosen, is still young and inexperienced; the work, however, is great, for this palace is not meant for human beings, but for the Lord God.

text.NRSV: So I have provided for the house of my God, so far as I was able, the gold for the things of gold, the silver for the things of silver, the bronze for the things of bronze, the iron for the things of iron, and wood for the things of wood, besides great quantities of onyx and stones for setting, antimony, colored stones, all sorts of precious stones, and marble in abundance.
text.Rotherham: And, with all my might, have I made preparation for the house of my God, the gold for the gold, and the silver for the silver, and the bronze for the bronze, the iron for the iron, and the wood for the wood,—and beryl stones and settings, stones coloured and particoloured, and all manner of precious stones and stones of white marble, in abundance.
text.KJV: 29:2 Now I haue prepared with all my might for the house of my God, the gold for things to be made of gold, the siluer for things of siluer, and the brasse for things of brasse, the yron for things of yron, and wood for things of wood, onix stones, and stones to be set, glistering stones, and of diuers colours, and all maner of precious stones, and marble stones in abundance.
text.NABRE: 29:2 For this reason I have stored up for the house of my God, as far as I was able, gold for what will be made of gold, silver for what will be made of silver, bronze for what will be made of bronze, iron for what will be made of iron, wood for what will be made of wood, onyx stones and settings for them, carnelian and mosaic stones, every other kind of precious stone, and great quantities of marble.

text.NRSV: Moreover, in addition to all that I have provided for the holy house, I have a treasure of my own of gold and silver, and because of my devotion to the house of my God I give it to the house of my God:
text.Rotherham: And yet further, because of my delighting in the house of my God, having a treasure of mine own in gold and silver, I have given for the house of my God, above and beyond all that I have prepared for the holy house:
text.KJV: 29:3 Moreouer, because I haue set my affection to the house of my God, I haue of mine owne proper good, of gold and siluer, which I haue giuen to the house of my God, ouer & aboue all that I haue prepared for the holy house:
text.NABRE: 29:3 But now, because of the delight I take in the house of my God, in addition to all that I stored up for the holy house, I give to the house of my God my personal fortune in gold and silver:

text.NRSV: three thousand talents of gold, of the gold of Ophir, and seven thousand talents of refined silver, for overlaying the walls of the house,
text.Rotherham: three thousand talents of gold, of the gold of Ophir,—and seven thousand talents of refined silver, for overlaying the walls of the recesses.
text.KJV: 29:4 Euen three thousand talents of gold, of the gold of Ophir, and seuen thousand talents of refined siluer, to ouerlay the walles of the houses withall.
text.NABRE: 29:4 three thousand talents of Ophir gold, and seven thousand talents of refined silver, for overlaying the walls of the rooms,

text.NRSV: and for all the work to be done by artisans, gold for the things of gold and silver for the things of silver. Who then will offer willingly, consecrating themselves today to the Lord?”
text.Rotherham: Of gold for the gold, and of silver for the silver, and for all manner of work in the hand of artificers,—Who then is ready to offer willingly, by filling his hand to-day, unto Yahweh?
text.KJV: 29:5 The gold for things of golde, and the siluer for things of siluer, and for all maner of worke to be made by the hands of Artificers. And who then is willing to consecrate his seruice this day vnto the Lord?
text.NABRE: 29:5 for the various utensils to be made of gold and silver, and for every work that is to be done by artisans. Now, who else will contribute generously and consecrate themselves this day to the Lord?”

text.NRSV: Then the heads of ancestral houses made their freewill offerings, as did also the leaders of the tribes, the commanders of the thousands and of the hundreds, and the officers over the king’s work.
text.Rotherham: Then offered they willingly—the ancestral rulers and the rulers of the tribes of Israel, and the rulers of thousands and hundreds, even to the rulers of the work of the king;
text.KJV: 29:6 ¶ Then the chiefe of the fathers and Princes of the tribes of Israel, and the captaines of thousands and of hundreds, with the rulers ouer the Kings worke, offered willingly,
text.NABRE: 29:6 Then the heads of the families, the tribal commanders of Israel, the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and those who had command of the king’s affairs came forward willingly

text.NRSV: They gave for the service of the house of God five thousand talents and ten thousand darics of gold, ten thousand talents of silver, eighteen thousand talents of bronze, and one hundred thousand talents of iron.
text.Rotherham: and gave for the service of the house of God, of gold, five thousand talents and ten thousand drams, and, of silver, ten thousand talents, and, of bronze, eighteen thousand talents,—and, of iron, one hundred thousand talents.
text.KJV: 29:7 And gaue for the seruice of the house of God, of gold fiue thousand talents, and ten thousand drammes: and of siluer, ten thousand talents: and of brasse, eighteene thousand talents: and one hundred thousand talents of yron.
text.NABRE: 29:7 and contributed for the service of the house of God five thousand talents and ten thousand darics of gold, ten thousand talents of silver, eighteen thousand talents of bronze, and one hundred thousand talents of iron.

text.NRSV: Whoever had precious stones gave them to the treasury of the house of the Lord, into the care of Jehiel the Gershonite.
text.Rotherham: And, every one with whom were found precious stones, gave to the treasure of the house of Yahweh,—unto the hand of Jehiel the Gershonite.
text.KJV: 29:8 And they with whom precious stones were found, gaue them to the treasure of the house of the Lord, by the hand of Iehiel the Gershonite.
text.NABRE: 29:8 Those who had precious stones gave them into the keeping of Jehiel the Gershonite for the treasury of the house of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Then the people rejoiced because these had given willingly, for with a whole heart they had offered freely to the Lord; King David also rejoiced greatly.
text.Rotherham: So the people rejoiced because they willingly offered, for, with a perfect heart, offered they willingly unto Yahweh,—moreover also, David the king, rejoiced with great joy.
text.KJV: 29:9 Then the people reioyced, for that they offred willingly, because with perfect heart they offered willingly to the Lord: and Dauid the King also reioyced with great ioy.
text.NABRE: 29:9 The people rejoiced over these free-will offerings, for they had been contributed to the Lord wholeheartedly. King David also rejoiced greatly.

text.NRSV: Then David blessed the Lord in the presence of all the assembly; David said: “Blessed are you, O Lord, the God of our ancestor Israel, forever and ever.
text.Rotherham: Therefore did David bless Yahweh in the eyes of all the convocation,—and David said, Blessed art thou, O Yahweh, the God of Israel our father, from age even unto age.
text.KJV: 29:10 ¶ Wherefore Dauid blessed the Lord before all the Congregation: and Dauid saide, Blessed bee thou, Lord God of Israel our father, for euer and euer.
text.NABRE: 29:10 David’s Prayer. Then David blessed the Lord in the sight of the whole assembly. David said: “Blessed are you, Lord, God of Israel our father, from eternity to eternity.

text.NRSV: Yours, O Lord, are the greatness, the power, the glory, the victory, and the majesty; for all that is in the heavens and on the earth is yours; yours is the kingdom, O Lord, and you are exalted as head above all.
text.Rotherham: Thine, O Yahweh, are Greatness, and Might, and Beauty, and Victory, and Majesty, nay! all in the heavens and in the earth,—thine, O Yahweh, is the kingdom, who art exalted above all,—as chief;
text.KJV: 29:11 Thine, O Lord, is the greatnes, and the power, and the glory, & the victorie, and the maiestie: for all that is in the heauen & in the earth, is thine: thine is the kingdome, O Lord, and thou art exalted as head aboue all.
text.NABRE: 29:11 Yours, Lord, are greatness and might, majesty, victory, and splendor. For all in heaven and on earth is yours; yours, Lord, is kingship; you are exalted as head over all.
